meta.title: THE PASTORAL RECORD
meta.subtitle: A six-week witness account — do not read ahead
meta.inspiration: A priest in a small hotel town discovers something arrived in the building years ago and has been waiting; the congregation has been accumulating testimony ever since, unsure whether proof will arrive before the thing renders the question irrelevant.
meta.author: Iron & Aether Engine v2

setup.title: THE REGISTER OF WEIGHTS — INITIAL ASSESSMENT
setup.instructions: Before the first session: record your current working weights below. These are your baseline loads for Week 1 at 70%. At the conclusion of Week 6, increase Squat and Deadlift by 10 lb, and Bench Press by 5 lb for the next cycle. Pull Up progression: add 5 lb to the loading scheme each cycle.

1: What did we notice that we have not yet written down?
Which room on the ground floor smelled different this week?
2: The priest conducted the service as usual. Did anything in his face change?
Which detail from last week do we now doubt?
3: We are no longer certain the thing arrived recently. What does that mean?
Has the hotel changed, or have we?
4: We held still this week. What moved while we were not moving?
Which entry in this record do we now read differently?
5: The mission has a cost we agreed to before we understood it. Do we still agree?
What would constitute proof? Write the answer. Do not cross it out.
6: We have been in this town longer than we intended. What are we afraid to name?
If nothing is watching, who wrote this record?

workout.progressionNote: Week 1: 3×5 at 70% · Week 2: 3×5 at 80% · Week 3: 3×3 at 90% · Week 4: 3×5 at 75% (deload) · Week 5: 3×5 at 85% · Week 6: 3×1 at 95%. After Week 6 final session: add 10 lb to Squat and Deadlift working weights, 5 lb to Bench Press and Pull Up, for the next cycle.

dice.timing: Roll once after completing all sets in a lifting session. For conditioning sessions, roll once after completing all 8 intervals.
dice.note: The d10 is the only die used in this record. It does not reward effort. It does not punish failure. It reports what was already true.

clocks.name: TESTIMONY
clocks.flavor: Each session adds to the record of what we have witnessed. TESTIMONY does not measure proof — it measures accumulation. The hotel responds to accumulation. When TESTIMONY reaches 4: cross off the seal on the GROUND FLOOR ANNEX and read REGISTRY ENTRY 1. When TESTIMONY reaches 7: cross off the seal on the UPPER CORRIDOR and read REGISTRY ENTRY 2. When TESTIMONY reaches 10: cross off the seal on ROOM 11 and read REGISTRY ENTRY 3. Pre-fill the first box before your first session — the record was already started before you arrived.

clocks.name: TESTIMONY (threshold 2)
clocks.flavor: When TESTIMONY reaches 7: cross off the seal on the UPPER CORRIDOR and read REGISTRY ENTRY 2.

clocks.name: TESTIMONY (threshold 3)
clocks.flavor: When TESTIMONY reaches 10: cross off the seal on ROOM 11 and read REGISTRY ENTRY 3.

tracks.name: THE ACCOUNT

thresholds.effect: Each time THE ACCOUNT crosses a marked threshold (3, 6, 9): permanently move the VIGIL baseline marker one notch right. The midpoint for -W routing has shifted. Mark it with an X so you remember the new center.
THE ACCOUNT has reached 6. Move the VIGIL baseline one notch right again — the new center is marked. The -W branch narrows. What we once called doubt now has a different name.
THE ACCOUNT has reached 9. The VIGIL baseline shifts one final time. The priest has been standing in the same place for six weeks. The center is no longer where it was.

tracks.flavor: THE ACCOUNT records what the thing in the hotel has done during our absence. It never decreases. Conditioning sessions roll to advance it. When it crosses each marked threshold (3, 6, 9), the VIGIL baseline shifts permanently right — the priest's center of faith moves, and cannot be moved back.

tracks.name: VIGIL

thresholds.effect: CONVICTION — the priest has held. Read ending E-HELD.
TOTAL DOUBT — the priest has accommodated everything. Read ending E-DISSOLVED.

tracks.flavor: VIGIL is a track with CONVICTION at the left end and DOUBT at the right. The marker begins at the center (position 5). Spending 1 RESIDUE shifts the marker one notch left (toward CONVICTION). Spending 2 RESIDUE for room access shifts it one notch right (toward DOUBT). THE ACCOUNT threshold crossings also shift it right permanently. Before reading each session's PASTORAL RECORD entry: check the VIGIL track. If the marker is left of the midpoint, add -C to your entry code. If right of midpoint, add -D. If exactly at the midpoint, add -W.

resources.name: RESIDUE
resources.earnRule: Earned from conditioning sessions: roll d10 after completing all 8 intervals. On 5–8: add one tally mark to RESIDUE. On 9–10: add one tally mark to RESIDUE AND advance THE ACCOUNT one mark. On 1–4: advance THE ACCOUNT only — no RESIDUE earned.
resources.spendEffect: At session's end, you may spend RESIDUE from your tally. Spend 1: shift VIGIL one notch toward CONVICTION. Spend 2: immediately cross off the next sealed room on the grid AND shift VIGIL one notch toward DOUBT. You may do both — spend 1 for faith, then spend 2 for access — but VIGIL moves twice.
resources.note: RESIDUE does not accumulate beyond what the tally marks show. Cross off spent marks. Never erase — draw a line through spent tallies.

codewords.trigger: Circle UNSEALED when ROOM 11's seal is crossed off — whether through TESTIMONY reaching 10 or by spending 2 RESIDUE early.
codewords.effect: When ROOM 11's seal is crossed off: turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY. Check your VIGIL marker's position now. Do not spend RESIDUE before reading. Read the ending indicated by your current VIGIL position. The PASTORAL RECORD is closed.

codewords.trigger: Circle WITNESSED if you ever read the -W (midpoint) branch entry for any session.
codewords.effect: The -W branch becomes slightly more available — the rare path has been walked once. The congregation includes someone who stood exactly at the center and looked both ways.

endConditions.condition: Week 6 complete. VIGIL marker is left of center (CONVICTION territory). Room 11 sealed or open — the position of the marker is what matters.
Week 6 complete. VIGIL marker is right of center (DOUBT territory). The priest has accommodated the incomprehensible.
Week 6 complete. VIGIL marker is exactly at center. The player has the codeword WITNESSED circled. This is the rarest ending — the congregation that held the middle and saw both sides.
Week 6 complete. VIGIL marker is exactly at center. Codeword WITNESSED is NOT circled. The record ends without conclusion. The priest is still in the building.

exampleTurn.context: Week 2, Session A (lifting). You have completed all sets. TESTIMONY is at 3, VIGIL is at center (position 5), THE ACCOUNT is at 2.
exampleTurn.result: SIGNAL: tick TESTIMONY once (now at 4). TESTIMONY has reached its first threshold.
exampleTurn.then: Cross off the seal on the GROUND FLOOR ANNEX on the hotel grid. Read REGISTRY ENTRY 1. Your encounter code for this session is determined by VIGIL (currently at center = -W suffix). Read the encounter entry with the -W suffix. At session's end, decide whether to spend RESIDUE — shift VIGIL toward CONVICTION (spend 1) or force a room open (spend 2).
exampleTurn.note: The roll told you something changed in the hotel while you were working. TESTIMONY does not measure your effort — it measures accumulation. The hotel was already counting.

archiveRouting: TESTIMONY threshold 4 → GROUND FLOOR ANNEX unsealed → REGISTRY ENTRY 1 (archive section: registry-entry-1)
TESTIMONY threshold 7 → UPPER CORRIDOR unsealed → REGISTRY ENTRY 2 (archive section: registry-entry-2)
TESTIMONY threshold 10 → ROOM 11 unsealed → REGISTRY ENTRY 3 (archive section: registry-entry-3)
Codeword UNSEALED → THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY → VIGIL position routes to ending E-HELD, E-DISSOLVED, E-WITNESS, or E-UNFINISHED
VIGIL -C suffix routes to CONVICTION branch of each encounter REF node
VIGIL -D suffix routes to DOUBT branch of each encounter REF node
VIGIL -W suffix routes to WITNESS branch — rarest, available only when marker is exactly at (current) center
replayability: Four distinct endings determined by VIGIL position and codeword WITNESSED — same six weeks, different spending choices
THE ACCOUNT escalation permanently shifts the VIGIL baseline — a second playthrough where you aggressively spend RESIDUE early will feel mechanically different
The -W (WITNESS) branch is rare and short; most players will not read it on a first playthrough
Spending 2 RESIDUE to force ROOM 11 early (week 5) vs. waiting for TESTIMONY to reach 10 naturally creates meaningfully different arcs through the hotel grid

map.title: THE HOTEL — PASTORAL REGISTER OF ROOMS

content.text: We did not come here to understand it. We came because the priest asked us to, and we had not yet learned to say no to someone with that kind of certainty in their eyes.
content.attribution: — margin annotation, undated, found on the inner cover of this record

content.text: ROOM 11. DO NOT ENTER UNTIL THE RECORD IS COMPLETE.
content.attribution: — stamped in red, front desk registry, The Hotel, undated

content.flavor: The priest conducted the morning service. No one spoke afterward. We counted the chairs.

encounters.title: FIRST ENTRY — ARRIVAL IN REGISTER
encounters.narrative:
We arrived on a Tuesday. The hotel lobby smelled of coal oil and something underneath it — a low, sweet smell like overripe fruit that does not belong to any fruit we know. The front desk clerk did not look up when we signed in. The register already had our names.

The priest stood at the window overlooking the parking lot for eleven minutes. We counted. He did not say what he was looking at.
encounters.challenge: The first session establishes what we are capable of. The weights tell us where we stand.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The afternoon light in the lobby shifted. We turned to look and there was nothing — only the clerk's pen scratching in the register.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We heard something move in the ceiling above Room 7. A single drag, then stillness. The priest said: write it down.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Nothing happened. This is also data. We wrote it down anyway, the way the priest taught us — absence is a kind of presence, if you hold still long enough.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The lobby clock stopped at 3:17. Not wound down — stopped. The second hand trembled against the same second for four minutes before the clerk reached behind the desk and did something we could not see.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: We pre-filled one box on TESTIMONY before the session. The record had already started. — The Annotator

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — FIRST CIRCUIT LOG
encounters.narrative:
The road outside town runs dead straight for four miles before it bends at the grain elevator. We ran it in intervals — thirty seconds of everything, ninety seconds of nothing, eight times. The asphalt held the previous night's rain in the low places.

At the third interval, someone in our group said they saw the hotel lights blink from four miles away. We finished the circuit. We did not discuss what was in the hotel while we were gone.
encounters.challenge: The intervals reveal what the body can sustain. Eight rounds. We do not stop early.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: When we returned, the front desk register had three new entries below ours. The names were not legible. The ink was still wet.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We returned with something we cannot account for — a feeling of having been watched from the grain elevator direction. Not unpleasant. Specific.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Four miles of road. Eight intervals. The hotel was exactly where we left it. This is more reassuring than it should be.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The grain elevator light was on. It has been decommissioned for seven years. We agreed not to mention it to the priest until we had a second observation to confirm the first.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: Sprint sessions: roll d10. 1–4: tick THE ACCOUNT. 5–8: add one RESIDUE tally. 9–10: tick THE ACCOUNT AND add one RESIDUE tally. The worst news is both at once.

encounters.title: MIDWEEK — THE PRIEST'S SECOND BRIEFING
encounters.narrative:
The priest convened us in the hotel dining room after closing time. The overhead light had a brown tinge that did not match the bulb wattage on the package still sitting on the table. Someone had already replaced it.

He placed a folder on the table and did not open it. He said: the previous occupants of Room 11 filled twelve pages of notes before they stopped writing. He waited for someone to ask what happened to them. No one asked.
encounters.challenge: Midweek is when commitment becomes decision rather than momentum.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: After the briefing, the priest stood alone in the hall for a long time. The folder was still on the table when we went to bed. Unopened, but we noticed it was thicker than before.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The priest read three sentences from the folder, then closed it and said: the previous team was not wrong. They were simply unprepared for what being right meant.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The briefing ended without a central statement. The priest folded his hands. The silence stretched for two minutes. We have counted a lot of silences since arriving.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The folder fell from the table. The pages scattered. One page landed face-up. It was a floor plan of the hotel. Room 11 was labeled in a different hand than the rest.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — SECOND RUN
encounters.narrative:
The road again. Thirty seconds, ninety seconds. The grain elevator was dark. We noted this. At the sixth interval, a car we did not recognize passed us going toward town — not toward the hotel, but past it, continuing on the highway. This is probably nothing. We noted it anyway.

The priest does not run with us. He walks the hotel perimeter while we are gone. We have begun to wonder what he looks for when we are not watching.
encounters.challenge: Sustained intervals. The body memorizes the pattern even when the mind loses track of the count.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: We returned and found the priest at the far corner of the hotel, one hand flat against the exterior wall. He did not hear us approach. His lips were moving.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The car we saw during the sixth interval was parked at the far end of town when we returned. Rental plates. No one inside.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Clean run. The priest was inside when we returned, seated at the desk, writing something he covered when we came in.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The rental car was gone by the time we reached town. The priest was standing in the hotel doorway. He said: I told you they would send someone to check on us. We had not told him about the car.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: Before reading this session's PASTORAL RECORD entry: check VIGIL. Left of center = add -C to your code. Right of center = add -D. Exactly center = add -W.

encounters.title: FINAL OBSERVANCE — THE CLOSED CORRIDOR
encounters.narrative:
The ground floor corridor past the dining room was locked with a chain and a padlock that was not on any key ring the clerk owned. The priest said he had tried the door. He said this casually, during breakfast, between sentences about the weather.

We looked at the corridor from the dining room all evening. We did not go closer. We told ourselves this was because we were following protocol. We were following protocol.
encounters.challenge: The final session of the week. The body knows it can rest tomorrow. This is where commitment costs most.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The chain rattled once at 11pm. We heard it from three different rooms, which means three of us were still awake at 11pm, watching the corridor from our separate doorways.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The priest spent twenty minutes photographing the padlock with a disposable camera. He has not mentioned the photographs since.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We spent the evening writing up our separate accounts of the week. The priest collected them at 9pm. He read them in order, then placed them in the folder without comment.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: Someone had slipped a note under the priest's door: a single sentence in a typeface none of our equipment produced. The corridor has always been open. The padlock was gone in the morning.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: End of Week 1. Check: TESTIMONY should be between 1 and 4. THE ACCOUNT between 0 and 4. VIGIL at or near center. Spend RESIDUE now if you choose.

encounters.title: SECOND WEEK — THE ACCOUNT OPENS
encounters.narrative:
Week two. The priest arrived at breakfast with the folder already open, already reading. He ate nothing. The dining room smelled different than the week before — the coal oil smell was gone, replaced by something drier, like warm paper or electronics left on too long.

We noticed that the photographs from the disposable camera were clipped to three of the pages inside the folder. We could not see them clearly. The priest did not offer to show us.
encounters.challenge: The weight has increased. The body remembers last week and does not want to believe this week is harder.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: After the morning session, the priest said: THE ACCOUNT is now open. He said it the way you announce a meeting has started. We wrote it down.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The photographs showed the padlock clearly. In the background of each photograph, the corridor behind the padlock was lit from an interior source. The lock was on the outside. The light was on the inside.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The priest reviewed the photographs alone for forty minutes, then said they were inconclusive. He used the word inconclusive with the care of someone who has been instructed never to say impossible.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: One photograph showed the corridor from inside looking out. The padlock was visible from the interior side. None of us took a photograph from inside the corridor. The corridor was locked.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: THE ACCOUNT escalation wire activates this week. Each time THE ACCOUNT crosses 3, 6, or 9: permanently shift VIGIL's center marker one notch right.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — THIRD RUN
encounters.narrative:
The rental car was back. Parked in front of the diner on Main Street. We ran past it twice during the intervals. On the second pass, someone was sitting inside — a shape behind reflective glass. We did not stop. Protocol says: do not engage unknown observers during active sessions.

The priest had written protocol in the folder margin next to a circled date three years before we arrived. We do not know what the date refers to.
encounters.challenge: Eight intervals. The shape in the car did not move as we passed.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The rental car was gone when we finished. The diner owner said it had been parked there since last week. She said this without looking up from the register.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We returned with the specific quality of attention you develop when you have been observed and cannot confirm it. We added this to the record.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Clean intervals. No incidents. The shape in the car was probably a headrest. We wrote it down as probably a headrest and moved on.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: On the final interval, we passed the car and the window was down. There was a folded piece of paper on the passenger seat. Our group name — the name only the priest uses for us — was written on the outside of the fold.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: If THE ACCOUNT crosses 3 this week: move VIGIL baseline one notch right now. Mark the new center with an X.

encounters.title: MIDWEEK — THE FOLDER EXPANDS
encounters.narrative:
The folder was now two folders, rubber-banded together. The priest did not explain when this happened. We counted: forty-one pages, eleven photographs, one hand-drawn floor plan with rooms numbered in a sequence that did not match the hotel's actual layout, and a single loose page in different handwriting that ended mid-sentence.

The priest removed the loose page before we could read it entirely. He said it was not relevant to the current phase of the investigation. We have learned not to ask what phase we are in.
encounters.challenge: Midweek accumulation. The body has committed. The question is what the commitment is for.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The hand-drawn floor plan showed a room between Room 10 and Room 12 that did not appear in the hotel registry. The priest numbered it 11a. He would not say more.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The photographs showed a progression — the same angle of the same corridor, across what appeared to be weeks or months. The light in the corridor grew brighter in each successive image.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We read the forty-one pages carefully and found seven inconsistencies — dates that could not both be correct, measurements that contradicted each other, a name that appeared twice with different spellings.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The loose page the priest removed: one of us had already photographed it on their phone before he took it back. The sentence that ended mid-sentence read: the room we cannot account for is not the room we were looking for — it is the room that has been
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: The previous team left forty-one pages. We are on page — [player writes current total here]. We will either finish what they could not, or add to what they left.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — FOURTH RUN
encounters.narrative:
Rain today, which made the intervals harder. We ran them anyway. The priest had asked us not to skip sessions — not as a request, as a statement of what we were going to do. We are beginning to understand this as his primary conversational mode.

During the third interval, one of us stopped at the grain elevator and stood looking at it for the remaining time. When we recovered, she said: there is a door on the side that does not appear in the county records. We continued the circuit.
encounters.challenge: Rain intervals. Grip is different. Footing is different. The body finds ways.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: We passed the grain elevator four times. The door was there on all four passes. The county records we checked later showed the elevator was demolished in 2016.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The rain brought a smell up from the road — the same low sweet smell from the hotel lobby on arrival day. We noted the coordinates and the time.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We ran eight intervals in the rain and reached no conclusions. We are starting to understand that the absence of conclusions is itself a form of data.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The door on the grain elevator was open on our final pass. Inside: a single folding chair, a thermos, and a spiral notebook, face down. The thermos was still warm.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.title: SECOND WEEK — GROUND FLOOR ANNEX
encounters.narrative:
By the end of Week 2, the TESTIMONY record approached its first threshold. The Ground Floor Annex corridor — sealed since we arrived — had begun attracting the priest's attention in a way that differed from his usual surveillance. He now lingered there between sessions, his hand flat against the wall at approximately shoulder height.

He said nothing about this. We wrote down the hand position, the duration, the slight tension in his jaw. We are a record. We record.
encounters.challenge: End of the second week. The weights are heavier. The hotel has changed in ways we cannot fully articulate.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest's hand left a faint impression on the corridor wall — the paint slightly compressed, as if he had been pressing harder than it appeared.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The priest said during the evening review: we are very close to the first threshold. He was not looking at any of us when he said it. He was looking at the wall.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Week 2 closes. The record is coherent. The inconsistencies from the previous team's notes are starting to resolve — not into clarity, but into a different kind of pattern.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The wall of the Ground Floor Annex corridor was warm to the touch. Not hot. Warm, the way a room is warm after someone has been sleeping in it.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: TESTIMONY has reached 4. Cross off the seal on the GROUND FLOOR ANNEX on the hotel grid. Read REGISTRY ENTRY 1 now.
THE ACCOUNT has crossed 3. Move the VIGIL baseline marker one notch right. Mark the new center with an X.

encounters.marginalia: End of Week 2. Consider spending RESIDUE now. Faith does not accumulate from inaction — only from choice.

encounters.title: THE PRIEST AT NINETY PERCENT
encounters.narrative:
Week 3. The highest point before the pause. The priest arrived for the morning session with the folder open to a page we had not seen before — typewritten, on hotel letterhead, dated three years prior. He read from it aloud without introduction: the occupant of Room 11 has declined further interviews. She states that the preparation is complete and that those who have not yet arrived are already on their way.

He looked up. His face was the face of someone who has been waiting for a sentence to confirm something he was not allowed to believe until it was written down.
encounters.challenge: The heaviest week. Three sets, three reps. The body is at its limit.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest was certain, for the first time, in a way that excluded questions. Certainty has a specific texture — it crowds out the space where questions used to fit.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We added the typewritten page to our collective record. The handwriting in the margin — someone else's — said: she was not wrong about any of it.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The priest finished reading and sat down. He was silent for six minutes. We counted. He said, finally: I need you to understand that this is not a question of faith. This is a question of evidence.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The hotel letterhead had a room number printed at the bottom of the page where most letters have a phone number. Room 11. We showed the priest. He said: yes, I know. That is why we are here.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: Week 3. 90%. Three reps instead of five. The priest has been certain before. The certainty always costs something we could not name until later.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — FIFTH RUN
encounters.narrative:
The spiral notebook from the grain elevator was gone when we checked on the second day of Week 3. The folding chair and thermos remained. We added this to the record: notebook removed, chair and thermos remaining, date and time noted.

The priest did not ask about the grain elevator. We realized, on the third interval, that we had never mentioned it to him.
encounters.challenge: Eight intervals under a low sky. The grain elevator door was closed.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: On the return, we found the thermos had been moved from the elevator to the road shoulder, directly in our running path. It was placed precisely, not fallen.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We decided to tell the priest about the grain elevator. We reached the hotel and the priest was waiting at the door. He said: the elevator. Yes. I know about the elevator.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Eight intervals. No incidents. We decided not to mention the elevator to the priest. We are beginning to make decisions about what to include in the record. This troubles us.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The notebook was back inside the elevator on our final pass. It was open to a middle page. The writing was in the same hand as the loose page the priest had removed from the folder.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: We have been keeping a second record. Not formally. In our heads, the observations we have chosen not to write down. This is the record the priest cannot read.

encounters.title: MIDWEEK — THE UPPER CORRIDOR THRESHOLD
encounters.narrative:
TESTIMONY approached its second threshold. The Upper Corridor — the hotel's second floor hallway, sealed with caution tape and a laminated notice from the management — had remained undisturbed since our arrival. The priest said it would open when the record warranted it. He said this with his back to us.

One of us asked: warranted by whom? The priest turned around. His expression was one we had not seen before — not annoyance, but something closer to the expression of someone who has been asked a question they have been dreading for months.
encounters.challenge: Midweek, Week 3. The heaviest midpoint of the program. The body knows what is coming.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest answered: warranted by the accumulation of record. He meant TESTIMONY. He means the hotel responds to the record. The hotel is reading what we write.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: TESTIMONY reached 7. The Upper Corridor seal came off — caution tape cut, laminated notice removed, the priest standing in the doorway holding the tape without moving for sixty seconds.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We stood at the Upper Corridor entrance. The smell from the lobby — the low sweet smell — was stronger here. Not alarming. Not pleasant. Specific in the way that information is specific.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The laminated notice on the Upper Corridor had been printed on hotel letterhead. It was addressed to the priest. By name. The management had addressed a corridor notice to someone who arrived three weeks ago.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: TESTIMONY has reached 7. Cross off the seal on the UPPER CORRIDOR on the hotel grid. Read REGISTRY ENTRY 2 now.

encounters.marginalia: The priest has been addressed by name by the hotel. We have been brought here by someone who was expected here. These may be the same fact.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — SIXTH RUN
encounters.narrative:
We ran the intervals with the knowledge that the Upper Corridor was now open. The priest stayed behind. He said he was going to walk the second floor. He said it the way you say you are going to take a shower — a routine maintenance statement that should not require explanation.

At the fifth interval, all of us reached the turn point simultaneously, looked at each other, and without discussion ran the sixth interval as fast as we could.
encounters.challenge: The sixth run. The speed at the turn was not planned. The body decided.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: When we returned, the priest was descending the stairs. He said: it is not what I expected. He did not say what he had expected.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We heard the priest in the Upper Corridor on our final interval — his voice, reading aloud. From four miles away. We could not make out the words but the rhythm was liturgical.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: When we returned, the priest was at the front desk with the clerk, reviewing the registry. Two pages were turned back. He looked up and said: how many days have you been here?

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The priest was standing at the bottom of the stairs when we returned. He was holding a room key. Room 11. He said: it was under the doormat on the second floor. He said this without particular emphasis.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.title: THE THIRD WEEK'S END — BOSS ENCOUNTER
encounters.narrative:
The final session of Week 3. The 90% week ends here. The priest called a formal session in the dining room — all of us, all materials, the folder open on the table. He placed the Room 11 key in the center. He said: this is the decision point. We have two protocols available to us. The first is to proceed immediately. The second is to wait for the testimony to complete itself.

The key had a tag. The tag had a number: 11. Below the number, in the same typeface as the letter on hotel letterhead, two more words. The priest placed his thumb over them before we could read. He said: you will see those words when you are ready.
encounters.challenge: The hardest session. Three reps at 90%. After this, the deload. After the deload, there will be no turning back.

bossRules.title: THE DECISION PROTOCOL — WEEK 3 FINAL SESSION
bossRules.preamble: The priest has presented two paths. Before rolling for this session, you must declare which protocol the congregation is following. Your declaration affects the next three weeks.
steps: 1. CHECK VIGIL. Note whether the marker is currently in CONVICTION territory, DOUBT territory, or exactly at center.
2. DECLARE: Will you wait for TESTIMONY to reach 10 before opening Room 11 — or will you spend 2 RESIDUE now to force the door?
3. If TESTIMONY is already at 10: the door is already warranted. Skip to step 5.
4. If you spend 2 RESIDUE: cross off the next sealed room on the hotel grid. Shift VIGIL one notch right (toward DOUBT). Circle codeword UNSEALED only when Room 11 specifically is crossed off.
5. Roll for this session as normal. Apply the outcome. The decision is made — the priest will not offer it again.
bossRules.stakes: Forcing the door early preserves RESIDUE for other uses but costs faith permanently. Waiting preserves the VIGIL position but requires continued accumulation. Both paths lead to Room 11. The record does not say which is wiser.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest placed the key back in the folder after the declaration was made. He said: the record will determine the rest. Something in the hotel shifted — a pressure change, or a settling. We cannot say which.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The key is in the folder. The decision is made. TESTIMONY continues to accumulate, or does not — but the priest's face, for the first time, showed something adjacent to relief.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The dining room was quiet after the protocol session. We filed out individually. The clerk at the front desk said, without looking up: have you decided? We told her yes. She said: good.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The words on the Room 11 key tag, seen after the session when the priest stepped away from the table: Already open. We did not tell anyone. We do not know if this changes the decision we already made.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: Week 3 ends. The priest has placed a key on the table and asked us to decide. We are a congregation. We decided together. We will not all agree about what we decided.

encounters.title: THE DELOAD — THE PRIEST WAITS
encounters.narrative:
Week 4 is lighter. The priest seems to know this without being told — he moves through the hotel at a slower pace, pausing longer at windows, eating breakfast completely before opening the folder. The reduction in weight is not a rest. It is a recalibration.

He said this morning: the interval between the decision and the consequence is the part of the record that future readers will find most instructive. He said this to no one in particular. We wrote it down.
encounters.challenge: Lighter weight. Maintained precision. The body uses deload weeks to consolidate what it has learned.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The hotel is quieter this week. Not because less is happening — because the priest is quieter. Whatever certainty carried him through Week 3 has become something more internal.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We found the priest's handwriting in the margins of three pages from the previous team's notes. He had been through those pages before. He arrived here having already read them.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The deload is when we notice things the heavier weeks made invisible. The hotel staff have been the same three people for the entire duration. None of them have left the property that we have seen.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The priest's handwriting in the margins was in pencil. The previous team's notes were from three years ago. The pencil looked fresh. Pencil does not stay fresh for three years.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: Deload week. The record continues even when the weight is lighter. Some of the best observations come in the pause.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — DELOAD RUN
encounters.narrative:
We ran the intervals at a pace we could hold a conversation through, which we did not because there was nothing new to say and because speaking about the mission on the road felt wrong in a way none of us articulated until one member said: I don't want the road to hear what we're doing here.

We all understood what she meant. We ran the rest of the session in silence.
encounters.challenge: Lighter intervals. The recovery time feels generous. The body wants more.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: On the return: the grain elevator door was open again. The chair was there. The thermos was gone. A second chair had been added.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The road during deload week has a different texture. We notice things we ran past at speed: a mailbox with no name, a fence corner where someone has pushed wildflowers through the wire, a painted stone.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Eight intervals at conversation pace. No incidents. The priest said the deload was structural — that the mission required it. He used the word structural with the precision of someone who means something specific by it.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The second chair in the elevator was facing the first chair. Someone had arranged them as if for a meeting. The notebook was back on the first chair, open. We did not go close enough to read the page.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: This is the prime spending week. RESIDUE has been accumulating. Faith is a choice, not a condition. What do you spend it on?

encounters.title: MIDWEEK DELOAD — THE CONGREGATION DIVIDES
encounters.narrative:
For the first time, the congregation did not agree. The disagreement was quiet — the kind that spreads through a group as a change in posture rather than an argument. Half of us believed the priest had been here before, had read the previous team's notes in real time, had participated in whatever happened to them. The other half believed this was projection — that we were attributing to intent what was merely preparation.

The priest held his regular session as if nothing was divided. This is either reassuring or not, depending on which half you were standing in.
encounters.challenge: Midweek deload. The body is consolidating. The mind finds its own agenda.

options.label: WE CONFRONTED THE PRIEST
options.cost: Shift VIGIL one notch toward CONVICTION

options.label: WE SAID NOTHING AND KEPT WATCHING
options.cost: Shift VIGIL one notch toward DOUBT

options.label: WE DIVIDED — HALF CONFRONTED, HALF WATCHED
options.cost: No VIGIL shift — the congregation held its center

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: Whatever we decided, the priest received it without visible change. His face was the face of someone who had rehearsed this conversation many times.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The decision clarified something in the group. Not agreement — clarity. We knew, by the end of the session, what kind of congregation we were.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The priest said: the congregation that waits is the congregation that sees. He may have been addressing us. He may have been reading from the folder.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: After the session, we found a new page in the folder — dated today, in the priest's handwriting. It described the disagreement we had among ourselves. In detail. Before the session where he could have observed it.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: The congregation has divided once. It will divide again. Each division is a reading — it tells you what the congregation believed when it was under enough pressure to split.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — FIFTH WEEK RUN (DELOAD)
encounters.narrative:
The rental car returned. Parked at the grain elevator now, not at the diner. A figure stood beside it — not inside it — watching us run. We were under protocol and did not stop. The figure did not approach. When we passed on the second interval, the figure was gone. The car remained through all eight intervals.

On our return to the hotel, the priest was outside. He looked at the direction of the elevator and said nothing. We said nothing. We had agreed, silently, that this was one of the things we would not write down until we understood it better.
encounters.challenge: Recovery intervals. Full presence of mind. The body can afford attention to other things.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The rental car was gone by the time we returned through town. No figure. No evidence of a meeting at the elevator. The thermos and two chairs were still there.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We decided to write down the rental car. It went into the record — third appearance, new location, observed figure. When we showed the priest, he said: good. Keep watching.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We agreed not to write it down yet. This is the second observation we have withheld from the record. The record has gaps we made deliberately.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The priest, unprompted, wrote in the folder: rental vehicle. Fourth appearance. Figure observed. Noted by congregation. He had not been with us on the run. He wrote it anyway.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.title: DELOAD CLOSES — WEEK 4 FINAL SESSION
encounters.narrative:
The deload week's final session. The priest said nothing unusual during the morning briefing. He ate breakfast. He reviewed the folder. He asked two questions: how does TESTIMONY stand? and where is the VIGIL? We answered both honestly. He nodded as if confirming something he had already calculated.

He said: next week, the weight increases. The record will need to keep up. We did not ask what he meant by keep up. We have learned, over four weeks, that the priest's statements are complete — the part we cannot hear yet is still in transit.
encounters.challenge: The final deload session. The last easy day before the weight returns.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: THE ACCOUNT has been advancing regardless of our effort or inaction. We are beginning to understand that it is not measuring us. It is measuring something else that is also being measured.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The priest left for an hour after the session. When he returned, he was carrying a second key — smaller, older, with no tag. He placed it in the folder without annotation.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Week 4 closes quietly. The record stands. The congregation holds its position. Room 11 is still sealed, or it is not — depending on the decision made in Week 3.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The clerk at the front desk spoke to us directly for the first time: the room will open when the record says it should. She said this without context, without looking up, while we were walking past. She was reading from nothing visible.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: THE ACCOUNT has crossed 6. Move the VIGIL baseline marker one notch right again. The new center is marked. The -W branch has narrowed further.

encounters.marginalia: End of Week 4. The priest asked where the VIGIL stands. Now you ask: what do you want to spend the next two weeks becoming?

encounters.title: THE PRIEST MOVING AGAIN
encounters.narrative:
Week 5. The weight is back, heavier than before the deload, lighter than Week 3's peak. The priest arrived for the morning session moving differently — a quality of forward motion that had been absent during Week 4. His certainty had processed into something quieter and harder.

He placed a new page in front of us. It was blank except for a single sentence at the top: The room number was always eleven. He said: I want you to think about what that implies.
encounters.challenge: The return of weight after the deload. The body has consolidated. Now it grows again.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: We thought about what it implied for the entire session. The most unsettling implication is the simplest one: that eleven was not assigned to this room. It is what the room is.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The sentence implies that the hotel was designed around the room rather than the room being assigned in sequence. We checked: there is no Room 10 on any floor.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We spent the evening reviewing every document for references to other room numbers. Room 11 appears fourteen times. No other room number appears more than twice.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: We looked up the hotel's original construction permits. Room 11 appears in the permit application. The hotel was built around it. The application is dated four years before the hotel was built.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: The endgame wire is now live. TESTIMONY may reach 10 this week. You may also force ROOM 11 with 2 RESIDUE. The choice crystallizes here.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — SEVENTH RUN
encounters.narrative:
We ran past the grain elevator without stopping. The door was open. Both chairs were occupied — we could see shapes, seated, from the road. We ran. Protocol. We have been in this town long enough to have developed a relationship with protocol that is close to gratitude.

At the final interval, one of us stopped at the elevator. The chairs were empty. The second thermos was full and warm. She left it where it was and finished the run.
encounters.challenge: Seven runs in. The circuit is familiar. Familiarity is its own kind of test.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The elevator chairs were empty throughout. We have been running past them long enough that occupied chairs may have become a kind of hallucination of pattern. We noted this possibility.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The member who stopped at the elevator found a single page from the notebook on the first chair. She brought it back. It contained a list of dates. Three of the dates were from this calendar year. Two were from our current weeks.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We completed the circuit without stopping. Whatever is in the elevator has been accumulating evidence about us for as long as we have been accumulating evidence about it.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The page from the notebook listed our names. All of them. The dates beside each name were our dates of arrival. The final entry, at the bottom, was the priest's name. With no date — just a question mark.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: The thing at the elevator has been keeping its own record. We have been observed at least as long as we have been observing.

encounters.title: MIDWEEK FIVE — ROOM 11 APPROACHES
encounters.narrative:
TESTIMONY was approaching 10. The priest reviewed the grid that morning and said: Room 11 will open when the testimony warrants it — or sooner, if the congregation decides it has waited long enough. He left the sentence open. He does not repeat himself.

We looked at the hotel map. Room 11 was at the far end of the Upper Corridor. We had walked that corridor. The door looked like every other door. There was no smell, no light underneath, no unusual warmth. We have been telling each other this for three days.
encounters.challenge: The weight increases weekly. The record approaches its most significant threshold.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The corridor outside Room 11 was warm by evening. Not dramatically. Two degrees, perhaps. The kind of difference a thermometer catches before a person does.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We are one or two sessions from TESTIMONY reaching 10. The priest knows this — he has been counting along with us. His behavior has been shifting incrementally toward something we cannot name yet.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Nothing came from under the door. No sound, no light. The absence has become as compelling as a presence would have been. We have been here long enough that the nothing is specific.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The Room 11 key — the original one from Week 3 — was no longer in the folder. The priest noticed when we did. He said: it may not be necessary.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: TESTIMONY has reached 10. Cross off the seal on ROOM 11 on the hotel grid. Read REGISTRY ENTRY 3. Then circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY when directed.

conditionalInstructions.condition: Player chooses to spend 2 RESIDUE to force Room 11 open
conditionalInstructions.instruction: You have spent 2 RESIDUE. Cross off the next sealed room on the hotel grid. If ROOM 11 is now crossed off, circle codeword UNSEALED. Shift VIGIL one notch toward DOUBT.

encounters.marginalia: The priest has not said what he expects to find. He has never said. We realize we have been following someone for five weeks without ever asking where they were leading us.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — EIGHTH RUN
encounters.narrative:
The rental car was parked at the hotel when we left for the intervals. Parked in our spots. We ran anyway. The intervals felt different knowing someone was in the hotel with the priest — or without the priest, or instead of the priest. We did not know which.

We ran eight intervals and returned. The rental car was gone. The priest was at his desk, writing. He said, without looking up: our visitor confirmed what I had hoped. We can proceed. He did not explain what had been confirmed or who had confirmed it.
encounters.challenge: Eight intervals with an open question at the end of each one.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest's notebook was open on the table. One line was readable before he closed it: a name we did not recognize, with a date and the word confirmed in the margin.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We returned to a hotel that felt subtly more inhabited than when we left. As if a conversation had recently ended and its residue was still distributed through the rooms.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The priest was writing. He looked up once to confirm we had returned, then continued. Whatever was confirmed, it had given him enough to fill three pages.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The visitor's name — visible briefly in the open notebook — was the same name that appeared with two different spellings in the previous team's notes. The person who had confirmed things for the priest was from the previous team.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.title: FIFTH WEEK CLOSES — THE VIGIL POSITION
encounters.narrative:
The final session of Week 5. By this point, TESTIMONY has either reached 10 or is close enough to feel it. The priest called a brief session before the workout and asked each of us to write a single sentence: what we believed the mission was. He collected the sentences without reading them. He placed them in the folder.

He said: the answers will matter more in a week than they do now. They always matter more once the room is open.
encounters.challenge: Week 5 closes. One week remains. The body has almost everything it needs.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The sentences we wrote are in the folder. We no longer agree about what the mission is — but the disagreement has a shape now, which is more useful than the agreement we carried into Week 1.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: When we wrote our sentences, three of us wrote the same sentence. Not similar — identical, word for word, without coordination. We showed each other. The priest said: <em>yes. That is the part of the record I cannot explain.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Week 5 closed without incident. We have been here long enough that the absence of incident no longer reads as safety. It reads as preparation.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The priest read the sentences. His face changed while reading one of them — a specific change, a recognition. He looked up and said: who wrote this one? Nobody answered. We had all written it.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: THE ACCOUNT has crossed 9. Move the VIGIL baseline marker one final notch right. The center is now marked for the last time. This shift is permanent.

encounters.marginalia: Week 5 ends. Check your VIGIL position now. The ending that waits in THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY is visible from here, if you look directly at it.

encounters.title: THE FINAL WEEK — NINETY-FIVE PERCENT
encounters.narrative:
Week 6. A single repetition at the highest weight. The priest said nothing during the morning briefing. He sat with his hands flat on the table and looked at the window. The folder was not open. After eight minutes he stood up and said: we are ready for the final session of observations.

We were not certain who he was speaking to.
encounters.challenge: The highest week. One set, one repetition. This is what the entire record has been building toward.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The hotel was quiet in a different way than usual — the usual quiet was absence. This quiet was presence. Something in the building was listening to us prepare.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The priest reviewed the full folder during the morning. When he finished, he separated a subset of pages — seven — and placed them in a new, smaller folder. He labeled it: Testimony Sufficient for Purpose.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We have been here six weeks. The priest has not wavered, not visibly. The VIGIL has shifted — is in one territory or another — but the priest's external presentation has not moved. We have been watching the wrong meter.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The clerk handed us a checkout form at noon. It was dated for the end of the week. She had checked the box marked purpose of visit: complete. We had not checked out. We had not told her we were leaving.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: Final week. Three reps becomes one rep. The record collapses to its essential question. One repetition is all the body has left. One reading is all the record will permit.

encounters.title: INTERVAL CIRCUIT — FINAL CONDITIONING
encounters.narrative:
The final interval circuit. We have run this road forty-eight times. We know every low place where rain collects, every fence corner, the exact moment when the hotel comes back into view on the return. The grain elevator door was open. The chairs were gone. The thermoses were gone. The notebook was gone.

We ran past without stopping. We have been doing this long enough to understand that the presence and absence of objects in that elevator is information we will understand later, or never.
encounters.challenge: Eight final intervals. The road is familiar. The body knows exactly what this requires.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: On the final interval, we looked back at the hotel from four miles. Every window was lit. It was midday. The lights had not been on when we left.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We returned and found the priest standing at Room 11's door — upstairs, Upper Corridor, door closed. He was not doing anything. He was standing there the way you stand in a place you have been trying to reach for a long time.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We completed the final circuit and returned to a hotel exactly as we had left it. Whatever conclusion is coming, it is not in the approach. It is behind the door.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The hotel was lit from inside, every room, including Room 11. Including Room 11, which is sealed, which has no external power connection in the hotel wiring diagram, which has been empty for — the duration listed in the register was obscured by a water stain — years.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.marginalia: The road is done. Eight intervals. Forty-eight sessions. The record has been accumulating since before we arrived. We are going to finish it.

encounters.title: SECOND-TO-LAST — THE PRIEST AT THE DOOR
encounters.narrative:
The second-to-last lifting session. The priest spoke during the morning briefing for nineteen minutes about a theory of cosmic indifference — not as theology, but as operational framework. He said: the thing in the hotel does not care about the mission. It does not have opinions about the mission. This is not comforting. This is the most accurate thing I can tell you about what we are going to find.

He paused and then said: the record will tell us whether we were right. The record does not know yet. Neither do we.
encounters.challenge: The second-to-last session. One session after this, then the reckoning.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The cosmic indifference lecture was the most honest thing the priest had said in six weeks. It was also the moment we understood that his doubt and his faith were the same thing, approached from different directions.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: After the session, the priest went to Room 11's door and stood there again. This time he placed his hand on the knob. He did not turn it. We wrote this down.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: The priest sat alone in the dining room after dinner until we could no longer stay awake to watch. In the morning, there were seven new pages in the folder. He had been writing his conclusions.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The priest said, at the end of the briefing: I have been here before. Not this hotel. This exact situation — this room, this testimony, this congregation. I have done this before. He said it with neither grief nor relief. As a statement of operational fact.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more.

encounters.marginalia: The priest has been here before. The room has been waiting. The congregation is new each time. We are beginning to understand what kind of record we are.

encounters.title: FOURTH INTERVAL — FINAL CIRCUIT
encounters.narrative:
The second-to-last conditioning session. We ran with unusual attention — the kind of attention you pay to an ordinary thing you know you will not do again in exactly this way. The road. The grain elevator, empty and open. The hotel at the turn point, every window dark now. The particular quality of light at the exact speed of interval running.

We ran eight rounds and returned and found the priest at the front desk, adding a final page to the folder. The clerk watched him without speaking. We watched both of them. The record noted all of it.
encounters.challenge: Near the end. The intervals have become a kind of ceremony.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: The priest's final page was a title page. Not a conclusion — a title. The folder was not named before. It was named now: The Pastoral Record.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: We ran the final interval and on the return the hotel was lit again — not every room, just one. The upper floor. Room 11. The light moved once, slowly, like someone turning in a room.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: Eight clean intervals. No incidents. We returned. The priest nodded. We stood in the lobby and understood that the next session was the final one and that we had arrived here regardless of what we had believed when we came.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: The register at the front desk had a new entry at the bottom — our name, our check-out date, and a note in the clerk's handwriting: mission: complete (anticipated). The date was from a week before we arrived.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Add one tally to RESIDUE.

encounters.title: THE LAST PASTORAL SESSION
encounters.narrative:
The final session. The priest stood at Room 11's door. He was holding the folder. He had not opened it since naming it. We stood behind him in the Upper Corridor — all of us, in the order we had arrived, which is also the order in which we had become uncertain.

He said: I want you to understand that I do not know what is in this room. I have been told what is in this room. I have read what is in this room. I have organized six weeks of testimony about what is in this room. None of this constitutes knowing. This is the last entry in the record before the record becomes something else.

He opened the door.
encounters.challenge: One repetition. The highest weight. The body at its single point of maximum capacity.

outcomes.name: STATIC
outcomes.text: What we found in Room 11 is in the record. It has always been in the record. THE ACCOUNT tells you what was accumulating. VIGIL tells you what it cost. The room simply confirmed what the weeks had already written.

outcomes.name: SIGNAL
outcomes.text: The testimony was sufficient. The room opened. The priest stood in the doorway for a long time and then stepped inside. We did not follow immediately. The record notes the gap.
outcomes.effect: Tick TESTIMONY once. Check codeword UNSEALED — if not yet circled, circle it now.

outcomes.name: SILENCE
outcomes.text: We opened the door and found exactly what the universe offers every congregation that has accumulated enough testimony: the room. The room was only a room. The priest had expected this. He had not warned us that expectation and experience are different.
outcomes.effect: Tick TESTIMONY once. Check codeword UNSEALED — if not yet circled, circle it now.

outcomes.name: INTERFERENCE
outcomes.text: Room 11 was empty. Not recently vacated — empty in the way a place is empty when it has never been otherwise. Nothing to account for. Nothing that had moved or waited. The priest stood in the empty room and said nothing for a very long time. Then he said: yes. This is what I thought. We did not know if he was relieved or not. We do not think he knew either.
outcomes.effect: Tick THE ACCOUNT once. Tick THE ACCOUNT once more. Circle codeword UNSEALED if not already circled.

conditionalInstructions.instruction: Room 11 is open. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY. Check your VIGIL marker position now. Do not spend RESIDUE before reading. Read the ending indicated by your current VIGIL position. The PASTORAL RECORD is closed.

conditionalInstructions.condition: TESTIMONY has not yet reached 10 and UNSEALED is not circled
conditionalInstructions.instruction: The door is open. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY. The record is complete.

encounters.marginalia: We are the congregation. We were always the congregation. The priest knew this before we did — before we arrived — before the record was started. This is the last entry. We are signing it together.

cover.classification: PASTORAL RECORD // INTERNAL CIRCULATION ONLY // DO NOT READ AHEAD
cover.subjectLine: Six-Week Witness Account — Hotel Property, Unincorporated Territory
cover.tagline: We did not come here to understand it.
cover.intro:
The hotel has been open continuously since the year of its construction, which the county records list as disputed. The priest arrived before us. The register at the front desk already contained our names when we signed in. The coal oil smell in the lobby dissipated by the second week and was replaced by something drier, like paper left too long in a warm room.

This is the record of what we witnessed. We are not the first congregation to keep it. We will not, the priest assures us, be the last. What follows is dated, stamped, and signed in the order in which it occurred — with one exception, which the record itself will make plain.

classifications.endings: THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY — SEALED UNTIL ROOM 11 IS OPEN

manual.title: FIELD PROCEDURES — HOW THIS RECORD WORKS

sections.heading: WHAT YOU NEED
sections.body: One ten-sided die (d10). One pencil. This record. Begin by filling in THE REGISTER OF WEIGHTS on the setup page, then pre-fill the first box of TESTIMONY — the record was already started before you arrived.

sections.heading: EACH SESSION
sections.body:
1. Complete all sets.
  2. Roll the d10.
  3. Find your result in the OBSERVATION TABLE on the HUD.
  4. Mark the indicated tracker (TESTIMONY or THE ACCOUNT).
  5. Check VIGIL's position: left of center = add -C to your REF code; right of center = add -D; exactly center = add -W.
  6. Read the PASTORAL RECORD entry for your REF code.
  7. At session's end, decide whether to spend RESIDUE.

sections.heading: YOUR TRACKERS
sections.body:
• TESTIMONY — fills toward 10. At 4, 7, and 10: a sealed area of the hotel opens. Read the indicated REGISTRY ENTRY.
  • THE ACCOUNT — only rises. At 3, 6, and 9: permanently shift VIGIL's center one notch right.
  • VIGIL — begins at center. Determines which PASTORAL RECORD branch you read. Shifts left (CONVICTION) or right (DOUBT) through your RESIDUE decisions and THE ACCOUNT's escalation.
  • RESIDUE — tally marks earned from interval sessions. Spend 1 to shift VIGIL toward CONVICTION. Spend 2 to unseal the next room and shift VIGIL toward DOUBT.

sections.heading: IF YOU MISS A SESSION
sections.body: Do not roll for missed sessions. The record contains only what was witnessed. Resume where you left off.

sections.heading: CONDITIONING SESSIONS
sections.body: Interval sessions use a separate rule: roll d10 after completing all 8 rounds. On 1–4: mark THE ACCOUNT. On 5–8: add one RESIDUE tally. On 9–10: mark THE ACCOUNT AND add one RESIDUE tally.

hud.diceNote: Roll d10 after completing all sets. Find your result. Mark the indicated tracker. For interval sessions: see CONDITIONING SESSIONS in field procedures.

archive.registry-entry-1Intro: Read when TESTIMONY reaches 4. Cross off the seal on the GROUND FLOOR ANNEX on the hotel grid before reading. This entry was sealed on arrival. The seal has now been removed by the accumulation of the record.
archive.registry-entry-2Intro: Read when TESTIMONY reaches 7. Cross off the seal on the UPPER CORRIDOR on the hotel grid before reading. The caution tape and laminated notice have been removed. The corridor is now accessible.
archive.registry-entry-3Intro: Read when TESTIMONY reaches 10, OR when you have spent 2 RESIDUE to force access. Cross off the seal on ROOM 11 on the hotel grid. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Do not proceed to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY until directed by the session record.
archive.evidenceIntro: Cross-reference entries using the prefix {{prefix}} followed by the week number, session code, and VIGIL suffix. The suffix determines which branch of the PASTORAL RECORD you read: -C for CONVICTION territory, -D for DOUBT territory, -W for the center. The -W branch is available only when VIGIL is exactly at the current midpoint.

endingsTrigger.heading: READ THIS PAGE ONLY WHEN CODEWORD UNSEALED IS CIRCLED
lines: Check VIGIL's current marker position before reading any ending. Do not spend RESIDUE after Room 11 opens.
VIGIL marker LEFT of center (CONVICTION territory): read ending E-HELD.
VIGIL marker RIGHT of center (DOUBT territory): read ending E-DISSOLVED.
VIGIL marker exactly at center AND codeword WITNESSED is circled: read ending E-WITNESS.
VIGIL marker exactly at center AND codeword WITNESSED is NOT circled: read ending E-UNFINISHED.

1.text: INTAKE — Before Session 1: fill in THE REGISTER OF WEIGHTS. Pre-fill the first TESTIMONY box. The record was already started. We do not know by whom.

2.text: THE ACCOUNT IS NOW OPEN. When it crosses 3 this week: move the VIGIL baseline one notch right. Mark the new center with an X. The shift is permanent.

3.text: WEEK 3 — MAXIMUM LOAD. The record approaches its second threshold. The priest is at full certainty. THE ACCOUNT and TESTIMONY are under simultaneous pressure. The decision protocol activates at Session 5.

4.text: DELOAD WEEK. The weight is reduced. The record continues. RESIDUE has been accumulating — this week is the prime window for spending. Faith is a choice, not a condition.

5.text: TESTIMONY APPROACHES ITS FINAL THRESHOLD. Room 11 opens this week if the record is sufficient — or sooner, if 2 RESIDUE are spent. The endgame wire is live. The VIGIL position you hold at the end of this week will be difficult to change.

6.text: FINAL WEEK. One repetition. The record closes. When Room 11 is open and codeword UNSEALED is circled: turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY. Do not spend RESIDUE before reading. The PASTORAL RECORD is closed.

1.text: The Ground Floor Annex corridor is locked. The padlock was not on any key ring the clerk owns. — Next session: the road outside town runs dead straight for four miles.

2.text: The photographs have been clipped to the folder. We have not seen all of them. — Next session: the folder has grown. Count the pages. Note the handwriting on the loose page before the priest takes it back.

3.text: THE UPPER CORRIDOR IS ACCESSIBLE. The priest has walked the second floor. He said: it is not what I expected. He has not said what he expected. — Next session: the key was under the doormat.

4.text: The congregation divided this week. The priest wrote down the disagreement before it happened. — Next session: the weight returns. What the priest means by structural will become clear.

5.text: The visitor has confirmed what the priest hoped. Three of us wrote the same sentence without coordination. — Next session: one repetition. The folder has been named. Read what the record has already written.

6.text: The record is complete. The priest named the folder. The clerk anticipated the checkout date. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY when codeword UNSEALED is circled.

3.alert: THE DECISION PROTOCOL — WEEK 3 FINAL SESSION

6.alert: THE LAST PASTORAL SESSION — ROOM 11 OPENS

finalPage.heading:
THE PASTORAL RECORD
IS CLOSED
finalPage.subline: Congregation dispersed. Accounts collected. Folder transferred to next custodian.
finalPage.instruction: If this is not your first reading of this record — if you have been here before — note on the inside cover which ending you reached and what the VIGIL position was. The next congregation will want to know.
