nodes.title: GROUND FLOOR ANNEX — INITIAL ACCESS REPORT
nodes.html:
DATE: [arrival +14 days]   FILED BY: Front Desk, M. Okafor   SEVERITY: ROUTINE

The padlock on the Ground Floor Annex corridor was removed as of 06:40 this morning. No record of key usage. No work order issued for the lock. The chain remains coiled on the floor at approximately 2.3 meters from the door frame. Management has been notified. No response received as of time of filing.

The corridor interior shows no signs of recent occupancy. Fluorescent tube in fixture 3B is functioning. The remaining three fixtures are not connected to hotel electrical. They are also functioning.

nodes.title: ANNEX — TEMPERATURE ANOMALY LOG
nodes.html:
DATE: [arrival +8 days]   FILED BY: Maintenance, T. Harrow   SEVERITY: ELEVATED

Wall surface temperature in the Ground Floor Annex corridor reads 31°C at shoulder height (1.6m from floor), tapering to ambient 18°C at floor and ceiling. Anomaly is consistent across a 4-meter span of the east wall. No pipes run behind this section per original plumbing schematics. HVAC does not serve this corridor — it was sealed from the system during the 2009 renovation. Source of heat is not identified. Anomaly has been present for at minimum six days based on staff reports. I am recommending we do not open this section to guests.

nodes.title: ANNEX — PREVIOUS OCCUPANCY RECORD (EXCERPT)
nodes.html:
SOURCE: Hotel Registry, Vol. 4, pp. 112–114   TRANSCRIBED: [undated, pencil]   SEVERITY: CLASSIFIED

Room 11a (unregistered): Occupied continuously from [date illegible] through March of the year prior to current filing. Occupant: F. Varga, listed under “extended research residency.” No checkout recorded. Personal effects removed by management on the 14th of April. One item could not be removed: a notebook, spiral-bound, blue cover, 96 pages. Current location of notebook: unknown. The room was sealed the following morning.

Margin note, different hand: She asked us to leave it. She said someone would need it.

nodes.title: ANNEX — STRUCTURAL ASSESSMENT MEMO
nodes.html:
TO: Hotel Management   FROM: Contracted Engineering, Solis & Brauer   RE: East Wall, GF Annex   SEVERITY: ROUTINE

Per your request, we have assessed the east wall of the Ground Floor Annex corridor. The wall is load-bearing and structurally sound. We found no evidence of moisture intrusion, settling, or pest activity. We did note that the wall appears to be approximately 40cm thicker than the building plans indicate — a discrepancy we attribute to either a plan error or a later internal modification not reflected in the permits on file. We do not recommend intervention at this time. We also note that our instruments registered intermittent compass deviation within 0.5 meters of the east wall. We have no explanation for this and are not including it in the formal report.

nodes.title: ANNEX — STAFF STATEMENT (F. YUEN, NIGHT DESK)
nodes.html:
DATE: [arrival +11 days]   COLLECTED BY: Day Manager   SEVERITY: ELEVATED

I have worked the night desk here for four years. The corridor has been locked for three of those years, since the incident with the previous guests. I have heard sounds from that corridor on eleven separate occasions that I logged in my personal notebook. The sounds are not mechanical. They are not the building settling. They are the specific sound of paper being turned.

I want it in the record that I reported this and that I was told it was the ventilation system. The ventilation system does not serve that corridor.

nodes.title: Letter from F. Varga to the Hotel Management
nodes.html:
Undated. Found folded inside the hotel's guest services binder, upper corridor housekeeping closet.

I am writing to inform you that I will not be conducting further interviews with your staff or with anyone your organization sends to speak with me. The preparation is complete. I have done what I was here to do and I am asking that the record reflect this: I was not wrong. I understood what I found. I understood it better than the people who sent me.

The room is ready. It has always been ready. What it is waiting for is not a key or a permission — it is a sufficient accumulation of witness. You will know when that has occurred because the record will have reached the point where the next entry writes itself.

Please do not open the corridor until then. The request is not for my sake.

— F.V.

nodes.title: Letter from the Previous Congregation Lead to F. Varga
nodes.html:
Carbon copy. Original presumed destroyed. Date: approximately 3 years prior to current record.

We have decided not to continue the investigation. I want to be clear that this decision was not made out of fear. It was made because we reached the point where continuation would have required us to accept a conclusion we are not authorized to accept. You told us the evidence was sufficient. The evidence was sufficient. We chose not to act on it.

I am enclosing twelve pages of notes. They are complete except for the final entry, which I left unfinished deliberately. I am leaving it for whoever comes next. I do not know if leaving it is the right decision. I know it is the decision I made.

I am sorry we did not go into the room. I think about it every day. I think you knew we would not go in. I think the room knew too.

— [redacted]

nodes.title: Letter — Undated, Unsigned, Found Under Door of Room 8
nodes.html:
Paper: hotel stationery, Room 11 letterhead variant. Typeface does not match any equipment on hotel property, confirmed by management.

The corridor is open now. You may walk it.

What you find at the end is a door like every other door. The door opens. What is in the room is what has always been in the room. We are aware that this description is insufficient. We offer it anyway because insufficient descriptions are more honest than comprehensive ones when the subject is what this subject is.

You are the fourth congregation. The priest knows he is the fourth priest. He has not told you. We are not certain whether this omission is protective or strategic. We have been watching long enough that the distinction may not matter.

— [no signature]

nodes.title: Letter from the Hotel Clerk to the Day Manager
nodes.html:
Dated: current week of record. Handwritten on hotel notepaper, left in the manager's inbox. The manager position has been vacant for eleven months.

I am writing this down because the priest asked me if I keep records and I told him I do and he said that was good. I have been at this desk for nine years. I have seen three groups come through for the same reason this group has come through. The first group did not open the room. The second group opened the room too early and left the same day. I do not know what they found. They did not check out properly. The third group reached exactly this point in the record and then something happened that I do not know how to describe except that the record stopped and they were gone the next morning and the room was as if no one had been there.

This group has the priest. The other groups did not have a priest. I think this is significant. I do not know why.

— M.O.

nodes.title: Letter from the Hotel — Addressed to the Current Congregation
nodes.html:
Found in the upper corridor, pinned to the wall with a single nail at eye level. The nail was not there yesterday.

You have come far enough into the record to read this. We want you to know that the record is accurate. Every entry has been accurate. The inconsistencies you found were not errors — they were the places where the previous congregations chose to stop writing. You can see the seams if you look.

The room at the end of this corridor is the same room it has always been. The number eleven is not arbitrary. There is no Room 10 because eleven was always first. The hotel was built around it. We think you know this by now.

We are not asking you to do anything differently than you have done. We are telling you that what you have done so far has been correct.

— The Hotel

nodes.title: ROOM 11 — PHYSICAL DESCRIPTION ON FILE
nodes.html:
SOURCE: Maintenance inspection report, filed [year -3], Inspector T. Harrow

Room 11 measures 4.2m × 3.8m. Single window, north-facing, permanently sealed with paint applied from the exterior (confirmed: exterior coat is not hotel paint — source unknown). One overhead light fixture, not connected to hotel electrical (confirmed: no wiring runs to this fixture). Light functions regardless. Floor: original hardwood, no damage, no wear patterns consistent with furniture or foot traffic. Walls: plaster, no marks, no damage. Temperature: consistent 22°C regardless of ambient exterior. One feature not present in any other room: a seam in the east wall at 1.6m height, running 90cm horizontally, consistent with a door frame that was plastered over. On the other side of that wall, according to all building schematics, is exterior air.

nodes.title: ROOM 11 — F. VARGA'S FINAL NOTEBOOK ENTRY (TRANSCRIBED)
nodes.html:
SOURCE: Spiral-bound notebook, blue cover, 96 pages. Recovered from grain elevator, Week 5 of current record.

Entry dated fourteen months after initial arrival:

The room does not do anything. I want to be precise about this because every account I read before coming here described something happening in the room. Nothing happened. The room is a room. What changed was not the room.

What changed was that I understood, standing in it, that the universe had been here before the hotel, before the town, before any of the people who had tried to understand what was here. The thing is not in the room. The room is a marker. It marks the place where the distance between here and everything else is thinnest. You stand in it and you know, without any ceremony, that you are very small and that being very small is not the same as being wrong.

I am not afraid. I am something that does not have a good word yet. I am going to try to write a better word before I leave. I do not think I am going to leave.

nodes.title: ROOM 11 — THE KEY TAG (TRANSCRIBED IN FULL)
nodes.html:
SOURCE: Physical key, brass, Room 11. Tag: hotel cardstock, laminated. Text below the room number, covered by the priest's thumb in Week 3.

ALREADY OPEN

Reverse of tag:

This key is not required for entry. It was made to give the holder something to hold. Previous congregations have reported that the physical object of the key made the decision easier to approach. We are not certain whether the key is helpful or whether it is a delay. We include it in the hope that it is helpful. The choice of when to use it — or whether to use it — has always been yours.

storyEndings.title: ENDING: THE PRIEST HOLDS
storyEndings.html:
You trained for six weeks. The weight increased and you met it, week after week, until the final single repetition at the highest load. The record is complete. VIGIL held in CONVICTION territory through the end.

The priest stood in Room 11 for a long time without speaking. When he finally turned around, his face had the specific quality of a person who has confirmed something they were told was unprovable. He did not say what he found. He did not need to. The finding was in the way he walked back down the corridor — each step deliberate, like someone crossing the same ground they have crossed before and knowing, this time, exactly where they are going.

The congregation filed out. The checkout was unremarkable. The clerk said safe travels without looking up. The folder stayed behind — left on the front desk, labeled in the priest's handwriting: For the next congregation. The record is sufficient. Do not start over. Continue from page one.

The hotel remains open. Room 11 is listed as available. The front desk register, at the bottom of the current page, already has the first entry for the next group.

storyEndings.title: ENDING: THE PRIEST ACCOMMODATES
storyEndings.html:
You trained for six weeks. The final single repetition at the highest weight was completed. The record ends with VIGIL in DOUBT territory — not collapsed, but shifted so far right that the center became unreachable sometime in the last two weeks.

The priest stepped into Room 11 and did not come back out for forty minutes. When he emerged, his posture had changed in a way that was difficult to name. He was not broken. He was not diminished. He had made a kind of peace that required giving something up, and the something he had given up was the part of him that believed the question of fate had an answer that would feel like an answer.

He said: it is exactly what I expected and completely unlike what I imagined. Those are different things. He said it the way you say a true thing that costs you nothing anymore because you have already paid.

The congregation left the following morning. The priest stayed behind for one additional day. What he did in that day is not in this record. When he checked out, the clerk did not ask where he was going. She already had the forwarding address on file.

storyEndings.title: ENDING: THE CONGREGATION THAT SAW BOTH
storyEndings.html:
You trained for six weeks. The final repetition was the hardest. VIGIL held exactly at center through the end, and the codeword WITNESSED is circled in the record — you walked the middle path at least once, stood at the place where CONVICTION and DOUBT are equidistant, and looked in both directions.

Room 11 was empty. It was also full. These were not contradictions.

The priest stood in the doorway for a long time and then sat down on the floor of the room and read the entire pastoral record from the first entry to the last. It took him two hours. We waited in the corridor. When he came out, he had nothing to say that was useful as language. He handed the record to the clerk, who placed it in a drawer behind the desk that already contained three other records in identical folders.

We left without a conclusion. This is the only ending in which the record itself disagrees about what was found. Four of us will tell you it was nothing. Four of us will tell you it was everything. The remaining member — the one who first walked the center path, the one who holds the WITNESSED mark — has not answered when asked. She says she is still thinking. She has been thinking for six weeks. We believe her.

storyEndings.title: ENDING: THE RECORD WITHOUT CONCLUSION
storyEndings.html:
You trained for six weeks. The final repetition was completed at the highest weight. VIGIL ended exactly at center. The codeword WITNESSED was never circled — you never stood at the precise midpoint and looked both ways. The record ends open.

Room 11 was entered. What was inside it is in the record. The record does not know how to describe it and neither do you. The priest stood in the room for twenty-two minutes by the clock in the hallway, which had stopped twice during the six weeks and was running again now. He came out holding the folder and said: the next entry would have to be written by someone else.

The folder is on the front desk. The final page is blank. The clerk says she left it blank on purpose. She says this is how the record has ended before — not with a conclusion but with an invitation. The next person to sign the register will be handed the folder before they reach their room.

You did the work. The record accumulated what it accumulated. The question of whether there is such a thing as fate remains open. This is not a failure. This is the condition the question has always been in. The hotel is still here. You are still here. The record continues whether or not you write in it.

R011.html: WEEK 1 / SESSION 1. The hotel register is open on the front desk. Our names are already there — not freshly written, but settled into the page the way ink settles after a week. The clerk's pen moves in slow circles over the next blank line.

R011-S.html: STATIC. The light through the lobby window changed angle while we were still inside. We had not moved. The afternoon did not move. Only the light.

R011-G.html: SIGNAL. Something dragged across the ceiling above Room 7. One sound. The priest said: write it down. He did not look up from the register when he said it.

R011-L.html: SILENCE. Nothing. We wrote that down too. Absence is a form of record, the priest told us on the drive in. We are beginning to understand what he meant.

R011-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The lobby clock stopped at 3:17. The second hand held against the same second for four minutes while the clerk reached behind the desk. We did not see what she reached for. The clock started again.

R012.html: WEEK 1 / SESSION 2. The road runs four miles to the grain elevator and back. Asphalt. Rain pooled in the low places. We ran it eight times in thirty-second intervals. At interval three, someone said they saw the hotel lights blink from four miles away.

R012-S.html: STATIC. Three new entries in the register below ours when we returned. Names not legible. The ink still wet — not damp, wet, the way fresh ink is wet when you touch it by accident.

R012-G.html: SIGNAL. We came back carrying a quality of attention we could not account for — a specific directional awareness, from the grain elevator side. We noted it. We did not discuss it.

R012-L.html: SILENCE. Four miles. Eight intervals. The hotel was there when we left and there when we returned. This is more than it sounds. We are learning what to find reassuring.

R012-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The grain elevator light was on. Decommissioned seven years ago — the hardware store owner confirmed this without being asked. We agreed not to mention it to the priest until we had a second observation.

R013.html: WEEK 1 / SESSION 3. The priest placed a folder on the dining room table and did not open it. He said the previous occupants of Room 11 filled twelve pages before they stopped writing. He waited for someone to ask what happened to them.

R013-S.html: STATIC. The folder was thicker in the morning than it had been at the briefing. We had all gone to bed. No one admitted to having touched it.

R013-G.html: SIGNAL. The priest read three sentences and closed the folder. The previous team was not wrong, he said. They were unprepared for what being right meant. He said this the way you say something you have been saving.

R013-L.html: SILENCE. The briefing ended without resolution. The priest folded his hands on the closed folder and waited. The silence ran two minutes. We have begun counting the silences.

R013-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The folder fell. One page landed face-up: a hand-drawn floor plan. Room 11 was labeled in a different hand than the rest — a hand that was not the priest's and did not belong to anyone in this building that we knew.

R014.html: WEEK 1 / SESSION 4. The road. Thirty seconds, ninety seconds. The grain elevator was dark. A car passed at the sixth interval going toward town — not turning in, continuing past on the highway. Probably nothing. The priest does not run with us. He walks the hotel perimeter.

R014-S.html: STATIC. He was at the far corner when we returned, one palm flat against the exterior wall. He did not hear us approach. His lips were moving without sound.

R014-G.html: SIGNAL. The car was parked at the far end of town when we came through. Rental plates. No one inside. We noted the color, the plate prefix, the exact location. The record grows.

R014-L.html: SILENCE. Clean run. The priest was at the desk when we came in, writing something he covered with his forearm. He looked up and said the intervals were going well. We do not know what he was writing.

R014-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The rental car was gone by the time we reached town. The priest was in the hotel doorway. I told you they would send someone to check on us, he said. We had not told him about the car.

R015.html: WEEK 1 / SESSION 5. The padlock on the Ground Floor Annex corridor is not on any key ring the clerk owns. She confirmed this twice when asked. The chain is wrapped twice and the free end hangs against the door at knuckle height. We have been looking at it from the dining room for three evenings.

R015-S.html: STATIC. The chain rattled once at 11pm. We heard it from three separate rooms — which means three of us were awake, watching the corridor from our separate doorways, and none of us had told the others.

R015-G.html: SIGNAL. The priest photographed the padlock for twenty minutes with a disposable camera — nine frames, we counted the advance clicks. He has not mentioned the photographs since. The camera is in his bag.

R015-L.html: SILENCE. We wrote up our separate accounts of the first week and passed them to the priest at 9pm. He read them in order, face neutral, then placed them in the folder. He did not say which of us had noticed the most.

R015-I.html: INTERFERENCE. A note appeared under the priest's door in a typeface none of our equipment produced: The corridor has always been open. The padlock was gone in the morning. The chain was coiled on the floor.

R021.html: WEEK 2 / SESSION 1. The photographs from the disposable camera are clipped to pages inside the folder, which is open on the breakfast table. The priest is reading and not eating. The dining room smells of warm paper now instead of coal oil. The coal oil smell has not returned.

R021-S.html: STATIC. After the session the priest said: THE ACCOUNT is now open. He announced it the way you announce a meeting has formally begun. We wrote it down. We write everything down.

R021-G.html: SIGNAL. The photographs: padlock, corridor, padlock again — and in each background, the corridor behind the lock lit from inside. The lock was on the outside. The light was on the inside. No one commented on this directly.

R021-L.html: SILENCE. The priest reviewed the photographs alone for forty minutes and said they were inconclusive. He used the word with precision — not unclear, not ambiguous. Inconclusive, which means the question remains open.

R021-I.html: INTERFERENCE. One photograph: the corridor from inside, looking out at the locked door from behind it. None of us took a photograph from inside the corridor. The corridor was locked when every photograph was taken.

R022.html: WEEK 2 / SESSION 2. The rental car was parked outside the diner when we came through on the first interval. Someone was seated inside on the second pass — a shape behind reflective glass that did not shift as we went by. Protocol: do not engage unknown observers during active sessions.

R022-S.html: STATIC. The rental car was gone when we finished. The diner owner said it had been parked there since last week. She said this without looking up from the register, the way you say a thing you have said many times before.

R022-G.html: SIGNAL. We came back carrying the specific quality of attention you develop when you have been observed and cannot confirm it. We have added this category to the record: probable observation, unconfirmed.

R022-L.html: SILENCE. Eight clean intervals. The shape in the car was probably a headrest. We wrote: shape, probably headrest, noted. The word probably appears four times in the record this week.

R022-I.html: INTERFERENCE. On the final interval the car window was down. A folded piece of paper on the passenger seat. Our group name — the one only the priest uses for us — written on the outside of the fold.

R023.html: WEEK 2 / SESSION 3. The folder is two folders now, rubber-banded. Forty-one pages, eleven photographs, one hand-drawn floor plan with room numbers that do not match the hotel, and a single loose page in different handwriting that ended mid-sentence before the priest took it back.

R023-S.html: STATIC. The floor plan showed a room between Room 10 and Room 12 not in the hotel registry. The priest labeled it 11a. He would not say when this notation was made or by whom.

R023-G.html: SIGNAL. The photographs are a sequence — same angle, same corridor, taken across weeks or months. The light in the corridor grows brighter in each successive frame. Eleven photographs. Eleven gradations.

R023-L.html: SILENCE. Forty-one pages, seven inconsistencies: two dates that cannot both be correct, three measurements that contradict each other, one name spelled two different ways. A record with seams.

R023-I.html: INTERFERENCE. One of us photographed the loose page before the priest retrieved it. The mid-sentence reads: the room we cannot account for is not the room we were looking for — it is the room that has been — and then stops.

R024.html: WEEK 2 / SESSION 4. Rain today. The intervals were harder. The priest had not asked us not to skip sessions — he had stated, as fact, that we were going to run them. We are learning the difference. At the third interval, she stopped at the grain elevator.

R024-S.html: STATIC. The door was there on all four passes. The county records show the elevator was demolished in 2016. The door has been there all four passes.

R024-G.html: SIGNAL. The rain brought something up from the road — the low sweet smell from the lobby on arrival day, rising through the wet asphalt. We noted the location: kilometer marker 2.4, outbound.

R024-L.html: SILENCE. Eight intervals in rain. No conclusions. The absence of conclusions is data. We are beginning to understand the record as a container for the negative space.

R024-I.html: INTERFERENCE. On the final pass the elevator door was open. Inside: a folding chair, a thermos, a spiral notebook face-down. The thermos was still warm when she touched it.

R025.html: WEEK 2 / SESSION 5. The priest stands at the Ground Floor Annex corridor wall with his hand flat against it at shoulder height. He does not speak. We write down: hand position, duration, slight tension in jaw. We are a record. This is what we do.

R025-S.html: STATIC. His hand left a faint compression in the paint — not a mark, a pressure, as though the wall had taken the impression of a hand held too long and too hard against it.

R025-G.html: SIGNAL. We are very close to the first threshold, the priest said during the evening review. He was looking at the wall when he said it, not at us. The first threshold is TESTIMONY reaching four.

R025-L.html: SILENCE. Week 2 closes. The inconsistencies in the previous team's notes are resolving — not into clarity, but into a different shape. The shape is beginning to resemble something we have not named yet.

R025-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The Annex wall was warm to the touch — not hot, warm, body-warm, the warmth of a room that has been occupied for several hours by something that holds heat. We did not stay long.

R031.html: WEEK 3 / SESSION 1. The priest read aloud from hotel letterhead dated three years prior: the occupant of Room 11 has declined further interviews. She states that the preparation is complete and that those who have not yet arrived are already on their way. His face was the face of a man who had been waiting for permission to believe something.

R031-S.html: STATIC. Certainty has a texture. It fills the space where questions fit. The priest reached it this morning and the dining room has been quieter since — not because less is said, but because certainty crowds out the need to ask.

R031-G.html: SIGNAL. A margin note on the typewritten page, in a hand that is not the priest's: she was not wrong about any of it. Someone had read this page before the priest brought it to us. The ink in the margin is older than the typewritten text.

R031-L.html: SILENCE. Six minutes of silence after he finished reading. We counted. Then: I need you to understand that this is not a question of faith. This is a question of evidence. He has not said this before. He will not say it again.

R031-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The hotel letterhead had a room number at the bottom where most letters have a phone number. Room 11. We showed the priest. He said: yes, I know. That is why we are here.

R032.html: WEEK 3 / SESSION 2. The spiral notebook was gone from the grain elevator. Chair and thermos remained. We logged: notebook removed, chair and thermos present, date and time noted. On the third interval we understood that we had never mentioned the elevator to the priest.

R032-S.html: STATIC. The thermos had been moved to the road shoulder when we came back — placed in our running path, not knocked there. Precisely placed, at the exact center of our lane.

R032-G.html: SIGNAL. We decided to tell him. We reached the hotel and he was in the doorway. The elevator, he said. Yes. I know about the elevator. He stepped aside to let us in.

R032-L.html: SILENCE. We decided not to tell him yet. We are beginning to curate the record — choosing what enters it and what does not. This is the first deliberate omission. We are noting it here.

R032-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The notebook was back inside on the final pass, open to a middle page. The handwriting matched the loose page the priest had removed from the folder. The same hand. The same pen pressure.

R033.html: WEEK 3 / SESSION 3. The Upper Corridor has caution tape and a laminated notice from management. The priest says it will open when the record warrants it. One of us asked: warranted by whom? The priest turned around with the expression of someone who has been dreading that question for months.

R033-S.html: STATIC. Warranted by the accumulation of record, he said. He meant TESTIMONY. The hotel reads what we write. The hotel responds to accumulation. We are writing to open a door.

R033-G.html: SIGNAL. The caution tape came off in one pull. The laminated notice was addressed to the priest by name. He stood in the corridor doorway holding the tape for sixty seconds without moving, the tape hanging from his hand like something discarded.

R033-L.html: SILENCE. We stood at the Upper Corridor entrance. The low sweet smell — the one from arrival day, the one from the road in the rain — was here too, stronger, and absolutely specific. Information in the form of a smell.

R033-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The laminated notice was on hotel letterhead, addressed to the priest by the full name he uses only in official correspondence. The management printed a corridor notice to someone who had not yet arrived when the notice was made.

R034.html: WEEK 3 / SESSION 4. We ran with the Upper Corridor open behind us. The priest stayed to walk the second floor. At the fifth interval all of us hit the turn point at the same moment, looked at each other without speaking, and ran the sixth interval as fast as we could. The body decided.

R034-S.html: STATIC. He was on the stairs when we returned. It is not what I expected, he said. He has never said what he expected. He did not say now either.

R034-G.html: SIGNAL. We heard his voice on our final interval — reading aloud, from four miles. The words were not audible. The rhythm was liturgical, the cadence of something recited from memory.

R034-L.html: SILENCE. He was at the front desk with the clerk when we returned, reviewing the registry with two pages turned back. He looked up: how many days have you been here? He asked it as a genuine question.

R034-I.html: INTERFERENCE. He was at the bottom of the stairs holding a room key. Room 11. It was under the doormat on the second floor, he said, without particular emphasis, as you would report finding a lost pen.

R035.html: WEEK 3 / SESSION 5. The Room 11 key is on the dining table. The priest has presented two protocols: proceed now, or wait for TESTIMONY to complete itself. The key tag has two words below the room number. He has placed his thumb over them. You will see those words when you are ready, he said.

R035-S.html: STATIC. The key went back in the folder. The record will determine the rest, the priest said. Something in the hotel shifted — not loudly. The way a building settles when the temperature changes.

R035-G.html: SIGNAL. The decision is made. The priest's face showed, for the first time, something adjacent to relief — the particular relaxation of a person who has been waiting for a choice and can now stop waiting.

R035-L.html: SILENCE. We filed out of the dining room one by one. The clerk at the front desk said, without looking up: have you decided? We said yes. She said: good.

R035-I.html: INTERFERENCE. When the priest stepped away from the table, we read the two words on the key tag. Already open. We do not know if this changes the decision we made. We have not told anyone.

R041.html: WEEK 4 / SESSION 1. Week 4 is lighter. The priest moves at a different pace — slower through the hallways, finishing his breakfast before opening the folder. The interval between the decision and the consequence, he said this morning, is the part of the record that future readers will find most instructive.

R041-S.html: STATIC. Quieter this week — not because the hotel is less active, but because the priest is. Whatever certainty carried him through Week 3 has turned inward. It is doing its work somewhere we cannot observe.

R041-G.html: SIGNAL. His handwriting is in the margins of three pages from the previous team's notes — pencil, recent-looking. He arrived here having already read these pages. He prepared before arriving.

R041-L.html: SILENCE. The hotel staff. Three people, same three every day, none of them seen leaving the property in four weeks. We have been cataloguing their movements and there are gaps — hours where none of the three appear anywhere we can observe.

R041-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The pencil he used in those margins. Three-year-old notes. The pencil looked fresh — bright graphite, no blunting. Pencil does not stay fresh for three years.

R042.html: WEEK 4 / SESSION 2. Lighter intervals. We ran them at a pace that would allow conversation. We did not have conversations. One member said she didn't want the road to hear what they were doing here. We understood her. We ran the rest in silence.

R042-S.html: STATIC. The elevator door was open on return. The second chair was new. The thermos was gone. A second chair, facing the first, arranged as if someone had been expected and the host had prepared.

R042-G.html: SIGNAL. A mailbox with no name. A fence corner with wildflowers pressed through the wire. A painted stone at kilometer 1.1. The road has been here as long as the town. The town has been here as long as the hotel.

R042-L.html: SILENCE. Eight clean intervals. The priest said the deload was structural — that the mission required it. The word structural implied the deload was written into the mission before we arrived.

R042-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The two chairs faced each other. The notebook was back on the first chair, open. We did not go close enough to read it. We are not sure this was the right decision.

R043.html: WEEK 4 / SESSION 3. The congregation divided today — quietly, as a change in posture rather than an argument. Half of us believe the priest was here before, read the previous team's notes in real time, was involved in whatever happened to them. The other half believe this is projection. The priest held his session as if nothing had divided.

R043-S.html: STATIC. Whatever we decided — the priest received it without expression. He has rehearsed this conversation. The face he showed us was the face he prepared before arriving.

R043-G.html: SIGNAL. The decision clarified something. Not agreement — clarity about what kind of congregation we are. We know now, and the knowing is permanent, whether or not we agree about what it means.

R043-L.html: SILENCE. The congregation that waits is the congregation that sees, the priest said. He may have been reading from the folder. The folder was open at the time.

R043-I.html: INTERFERENCE. A new page in the folder — dated today, in his handwriting — described our disagreement in detail. He was not present when the disagreement happened. He wrote it down before the session where he might have heard it secondhand.

R04C-C.html: WE CONFRONTED THE PRIEST. We asked directly: had he been here before? Had he read the previous team's notes before arriving? He looked at each of us in turn. Then he said: I am here now. That is what matters. It was not an answer. It was a boundary. We wrote down what he said, not what it meant.

R04C-D.html: WE SAID NOTHING AND KEPT WATCHING. Silence is a kind of accumulation. What we did not say to the priest joined the record of observations we have chosen not to formalize — the second record, the one only we hold. The priest continued his session without looking at us differently. This may mean he expected our silence. It may mean he did not notice.

R04C-W.html: WE DIVIDED. Half confronted. Half watched. The priest received both halves of us at the same time, in the same room, and showed neither half a different face. We are not certain this is reassuring. A person who has the same face for confrontation and for silence has practiced.

R044.html: WEEK 4 / SESSION 4. The rental car was back, parked at the grain elevator now. A figure stood beside it during the first interval. Gone by the second. The car remained through all eight. The priest was outside when we returned, looking toward the elevator. We said nothing. He said nothing.

R044-S.html: STATIC. The car was gone by the time we came through town. The two chairs and the thermos were still in the elevator. No evidence of a meeting that we could identify as such.

R044-G.html: SIGNAL. We wrote it down — third appearance, new location, observed figure. The priest said: good. Keep watching. He said this before we finished describing what we had seen.

R044-L.html: SILENCE. We agreed not to write it down yet. Two deliberate gaps in the record now. We are the record-keepers choosing what enters the record. This is the thing we were not told we would be doing.

R044-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The priest wrote in the folder: rental vehicle. Fourth appearance. Figure observed. Noted by congregation. He had not run with us. He was not at the elevator. He wrote it anyway.

R045.html: WEEK 4 / SESSION 5. The priest asked two questions this morning: how does TESTIMONY stand? and where is the VIGIL? We answered both honestly. He nodded once, as if confirming a calculation he had already run. Then: next week, the weight increases. The record will need to keep up.

R045-S.html: STATIC. THE ACCOUNT has been advancing regardless of what we observe or record or decide. It is not measuring our effort. It is measuring something else — something that is also, independently, measuring us.

R045-G.html: SIGNAL. He left for an hour after the session. He returned carrying a second key — smaller, older, with no tag. He placed it in the folder without annotation. The folder now contains two keys and neither is labeled for Room 11.

R045-L.html: SILENCE. Week 4 closes. The record stands. Room 11 is still sealed, or it is not — depending on the decision made in Week 3. We hold both possibilities until the record tells us which.

R045-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The clerk spoke to us directly for the first time: the room will open when the record says it should. She said it while we were passing, without context, reading from nothing visible. Then returned to what she was doing before.

R051.html: WEEK 5 / SESSION 1. The priest placed a new page on the table — blank except for one sentence at the top: The room number was always eleven. He said: I want you to think about what that implies. He left the room.

R051-S.html: STATIC. Eleven was not assigned. It is what the room is. Every other room in the hotel has a number that refers to its position. Room 11's number refers to itself.

R051-G.html: SIGNAL. No Room 10 on any floor. We checked. The sequence runs 9, then 11, then 12. The gap is not an error — it is in every version of the floor plan, including the original construction records.

R051-L.html: SILENCE. Room 11 appears fourteen times in the combined documentation. No other room number appears more than twice. The hotel has one room it cannot stop referring to.

R051-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The original construction permit lists Room 11 by name. The permit is dated four years before the hotel was built. The room was named before there was a building to put it in.

R052.html: WEEK 5 / SESSION 2. We ran past the elevator without stopping. Both chairs were occupied from the road. We ran. At the final interval one of us stopped. When she reached the chairs they were empty. The second thermos was full and warm. She left it and finished the run.

R052-S.html: STATIC. The chairs were empty throughout. We have been running past them long enough that we may have begun to construct occupants from the pattern. We noted this as a methodological concern.

R052-G.html: SIGNAL. A single page from the notebook on the first chair. A list of dates — three from this calendar year, two from our current weeks. Someone has been noting our sessions.

R052-L.html: SILENCE. We completed the circuit without stopping. Whatever is in the elevator has been accumulating a record of us for as long as we have been accumulating a record of it. The hotel is not the only archive.

R052-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The page listed our names. All of them, in the correct order of arrival. The final entry was the priest's name — with no date beside it. Just a question mark.

R053.html: WEEK 5 / SESSION 3. TESTIMONY was approaching its final threshold. The priest said: Room 11 will open when the testimony warrants it — or sooner, if the congregation decides it has waited long enough. The door at the end of the Upper Corridor looks like every other door. We have been saying this to each other for three days.

R053-S.html: STATIC. The corridor outside the door was two degrees warmer by evening. Not dramatic. The kind of difference a thermometer catches before a body does. We used the thermometer.

R053-G.html: SIGNAL. The priest's behavior has been shifting for days — incremental, directed, toward something we cannot name yet. He is preparing for something that the record has not yet described.

R053-L.html: SILENCE. Nothing from under the door. No sound, no light, no warmth at the threshold. The absence has weight now. We have been here long enough that the specific shape of the nothing is significant.

R053-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The Room 11 key — the one from Week 3 — was no longer in the folder. The priest noticed when we did. It may not be necessary, he said. He did not explain what the key had been for, if not for the door.

R054.html: WEEK 5 / SESSION 4. The rental car was in our spots when we left for the intervals. We ran anyway. Eight intervals with an open question at the end of each. The car was gone when we returned. The priest was writing. Our visitor confirmed what I had hoped, he said, without looking up.

R054-S.html: STATIC. His notebook was open on the table — one readable line before he closed it: a name we did not recognize, a date, and the word confirmed in the margin in a different pen.

R054-G.html: SIGNAL. We returned to a hotel that felt more inhabited than when we left. The quality of air in a room after a conversation has ended — that was what we walked into.

R054-L.html: SILENCE. He wrote three pages after the confirmation. He filled them without pausing. Whatever the visitor confirmed gave him enough to complete a section he had left open for weeks.

R054-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The visitor's name — briefly visible in the open notebook — was the name that appeared twice with different spellings in the previous team's notes. The person who confirmed things for the priest came from the congregation before ours.

R055.html: WEEK 5 / SESSION 5. The priest asked each of us to write one sentence: what we believed the mission was. He collected them without reading them. The answers will matter more in a week than they do now, he said. They always matter more once the room is open.

R055-S.html: STATIC. The sentences are in the folder. We no longer agree about the mission — but the disagreement has a shape now, which is a kind of progress. When we arrived, we agreed without having discussed it. Now we disagree having discussed nothing else.

R055-G.html: SIGNAL. Three of us wrote the same sentence. Not similar — identical, every word in the same order. We had not spoken. The priest said: yes. That is the part of the record I cannot explain. He said this without distress.

R055-L.html: SILENCE. Week 5 ends without incident. We have been here long enough that the absence of incident does not read as safety. It reads as preparation. Something is about to be ready.

R055-I.html: INTERFERENCE. He read the sentences. His face changed on one of them — a specific recognition. Who wrote this one? Nobody answered. We had all written it. We are becoming, collectively, something the record is producing.

R061.html: WEEK 6 / SESSION 1. The priest sat with his hands flat on the table for eight minutes without speaking or opening the folder. Then he stood and said: we are ready for the final session of observations. We were not certain he was speaking to us.

R061-S.html: STATIC. The hotel was quiet in a new way — not the quiet of absence, but the quiet of attention. The building was listening to something we were doing. We could not determine what we were doing that was worth listening to.

R061-G.html: SIGNAL. He reviewed the full folder and separated seven pages into a smaller folder. He labeled it: Testimony Sufficient for Purpose. Seven pages. Out of everything we have accumulated, seven pages were sufficient.

R061-L.html: SILENCE. Six weeks. The priest has not wavered — not visibly. The VIGIL has shifted, but the priest's face has been consistent. We have been watching the wrong thing. The priest is not the meter. We are.

R061-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The clerk handed us a checkout form at noon — dated for the end of the week, purpose-of-visit box already checked: complete. We had not told her we were leaving. We had not told anyone.

R062.html: WEEK 6 / SESSION 2. The final circuit. We have run this road forty-eight times. We know every low point, every fence corner, the exact moment the hotel comes back into view on the return. The grain elevator was empty. Chairs gone. Thermoses gone. Notebook gone. We ran past.

R062-S.html: STATIC. On the final interval we looked back from four miles. Every window in the hotel was lit. It was midday. The lights were off when we left.

R062-G.html: SIGNAL. He was standing at Room 11's door when we returned — Upper Corridor, door closed. Not doing anything. Standing the way you stand in a place you have been trying to reach for a long time.

R062-L.html: SILENCE. We returned to a hotel exactly as we had left it. The conclusion is not in the approach. Whatever is going to happen is behind a door we have not yet opened. We have one session left.

R062-I.html: INTERFERENCE. Every room lit, including Room 11 — which has no external power connection according to the wiring diagram, which has been empty for a duration the register cannot confirm because a water stain covers the date.

R063.html: WEEK 6 / SESSION 3. Nineteen minutes on cosmic indifference — not as theology, but as operational framework. The thing in the hotel does not care about the mission, the priest said. This is not comforting. This is the most accurate thing I can tell you about what we are going to find.

R063-S.html: STATIC. His doubt and his faith are the same thing. We understood this during the lecture — approached from different directions, they arrive at the same point. He has been holding them together for the entire six weeks.

R063-G.html: SIGNAL. He placed his hand on the Room 11 doorknob after the session. He did not turn it. He stood there for forty seconds with his hand on the knob, not turning it. We wrote it down exactly as it happened.

R063-L.html: SILENCE. He sat alone in the dining room until we could no longer stay awake to watch. In the morning: seven new pages in the folder. His conclusions. He had been writing them all night.

R063-I.html: INTERFERENCE. At the end of the briefing: I have been here before. Not this hotel. This exact situation — this room, this testimony, this congregation. I have done this before. Neither grief nor relief. A statement of operational fact.

R064.html: WEEK 6 / SESSION 4. We ran with the attention you give to ordinary things you will not do again in exactly this way. The road. The elevator, empty and open. The hotel at the turn, windows dark. We ran eight rounds and returned to find the priest adding a final page to the folder. The clerk watched him. We watched both of them.

R064-S.html: STATIC. The final page was a title page. The folder had not been named before. It was named now: The Pastoral Record. He wrote it in the same ink as everything else, as if it had always been the title.

R064-G.html: SIGNAL. On the final interval, the hotel was lit again — one room, upper floor, Room 11. The light moved once, slowly, the way light moves when someone turns in a room.

R064-L.html: SILENCE. Eight clean intervals. We returned. The priest nodded. We stood in the lobby and understood that we had arrived here regardless of what we believed when we came. The arrival was not the decision we thought we were making.

R064-I.html: INTERFERENCE. A new entry at the bottom of the front desk register: our name, our checkout date, a note in the clerk's hand: mission: complete (anticipated). The date was from the week before we arrived.

R065.html: WEEK 6 / SESSION 5. The priest stood at Room 11's door holding the folder he had not opened since naming it. We stood behind him in the order we had arrived — which is also the order in which we had become uncertain. None of this constitutes knowing, he said. This is the last entry before the record becomes something else. He opened the door.

R065-S.html: STATIC. What we found in Room 11 is in the record. The record already contained it — THE ACCOUNT told us what was accumulating, VIGIL told us what it cost. The room confirmed what the weeks had already written. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY.

R065-G.html: SIGNAL. The testimony was sufficient. The room opened. The priest stood in the doorway for a long time and then stepped through. We did not follow immediately. The record notes the gap between his crossing and ours. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY.

R065-L.html: SILENCE. The room was only a room. The priest had expected this — he had not told us that expectation and experience are different. We stood in a room that the universe had not arranged for us. We stood in it anyway. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY.

R065-I.html: INTERFERENCE. The room was empty. Not recently vacated — empty in the way of a place that has never been otherwise. The priest stood in it for a long time. Then: yes. This is what I thought. We do not know if he was relieved. We do not think he knew either. Circle codeword UNSEALED. Turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY.

intake.narrativeBrief: Small town — everyone knows, the past won’t die, secrets in plain sight. A hotel that functions as a world unto itself. The priest or spiritual leader — faith as vocation, the doubt they hide. They want to complete the mission, whatever the cost. Antagonist: The cosmic — the universe’s indifference, nothing is watching. Central question: Is there such a thing as fate? Narrative voice: Unreliable first-person plural — a ‘we’ that includes the reader against their will, collective guilt assumed. Tonal touchstone: Roadside Picnic — alien contact as environmental disaster, the human scrambling beneath the incomprehensible.

gameIdentity.coreLoop: Complete a session → roll for what we witnessed → mark TESTIMONY and THE ACCOUNT → thresholds unlock sealed rooms in the hotel grid and collapsed entries in the PASTORAL RECORD → the priest's VIGIL track tips toward DOUBT or CONVICTION, changing which rooms we may enter next
gameIdentity.primaryTension: Will our accumulated testimony constitute proof — or will the thing in the hotel render the question meaningless before we can answer it?
gameIdentity.discoveryMechanism: accumulate-and-unlock: TESTIMONY fills toward sealed room access; PASTORAL RECORD entries collapse inward as CONVICTION shifts; the hotel grid reveals what was always present but inaccessible

exploration.expression: The hotel is a Facility Grid (5A) with locked rooms. TESTIMONY threshold gates each sealed wing. The player marks which rooms they have entered and which still bear the red X of exclusion. The map IS the game — the act of crossing off a seal is the act of understanding.

progression.expression: Unlock chain: filling TESTIMONY activates a new sealed room cluster each phase. The PASTORAL RECORD (archive) expands backward — earlier entries become readable only after later ones, inverting the timeline deliberately.

choicesMattering.expression: Each session the player chooses where to spend RESIDUE — the accumulating resource. Spending RESIDUE on CONVICTION holds the priest together; hoarding it for ROOM ACCESS opens more of the hotel but costs faith. Every session is a choice between clarity and completeness.

immersion.expression: The entire booklet IS the PASTORAL RECORD — a parish document that has been interfered with. Entries are dated, stamped, crossed out, and re-annotated. The reader is implicated as part of the 'we' from page one. Every mechanic is named as something that would appear in a parish ledger or hotel registry.

wiring.to: Hotel Facility Grid — sealed room clusters
wiring.effect: At each threshold, one sealed room cluster on the hotel grid becomes accessible; cross off the red seal printed over those rooms and read the REGISTRY ENTRY for that cluster
wiring.printInstruction: When TESTIMONY reaches 4: cross off the seal on the GROUND FLOOR ANNEX and read REGISTRY ENTRY 1. When TESTIMONY reaches 7: cross off the seal on the UPPER CORRIDOR and read REGISTRY ENTRY 2. When TESTIMONY reaches 10: cross off the seal on ROOM 11 and read REGISTRY ENTRY 3.

wiring.to: Weekly encounter REF codes — PASTORAL RECORD branches
wiring.condition: Player checks VIGIL position each session before reading the week's encounter
wiring.effect: If VIGIL sits in CONVICTION territory (left of center), read the -C suffix encounter entry. If VIGIL sits in DOUBT territory (right of center), read the -D suffix entry. If VIGIL is exactly at center, read the -W (WITNESS) entry — the rarest and most complete branch.
wiring.printInstruction: Before reading this session's PASTORAL RECORD entry: check the VIGIL track. If the marker is left of the midpoint, add -C to your entry code. If right of midpoint, add -D. If exactly at the midpoint, add -W.

wiring.condition: Player chooses to spend RESIDUE at end of any session
wiring.effect: Spending 1 RESIDUE shifts VIGIL one step toward CONVICTION (the priest holds on). Spending 2 RESIDUE grants access to a sealed room without waiting for a TESTIMONY threshold — but VIGIL shifts one step toward DOUBT (the priest begins to accommodate the incomprehensible). Earning RESIDUE without spending it does nothing to VIGIL — faith neither grows nor decays from mere accumulation.
wiring.printInstruction: At session's end, you may spend RESIDUE from your tally. Spend 1: shift VIGIL one notch toward CONVICTION. Spend 2: immediately cross off the next sealed room on the grid AND shift VIGIL one notch toward DOUBT. You may do both — spend 1 for faith, then spend 2 for access — but VIGIL moves twice.

wiring.condition: THE ACCOUNT crosses thresholds at 3, 6, and 9
wiring.effect: Each threshold permanently shifts VIGIL's center marker one step toward DOUBT — the baseline of the priest's faith has moved. This cannot be undone by RESIDUE spending. The player's -C/-D/-W routing window narrows as DOUBT's territory expands from the right.
wiring.printInstruction: Each time THE ACCOUNT crosses a marked threshold (3, 6, 9): permanently move the VIGIL baseline marker one notch right. The midpoint for -W routing has shifted. Mark it with an X so you remember the new center.

wiring.condition: Player completes a sprint session and rolls d10
wiring.effect: On a 1-4: THE ACCOUNT advances 1 (the thing has been active — something changed in the hotel during our run). On a 5-8: gain 1 RESIDUE (we returned with something useful, we cannot say what). On a 9-10: THE ACCOUNT advances 1 AND gain 1 RESIDUE — the worst news. The player cannot choose the outcome; the dice determine it. But the player decides what to do with RESIDUE afterward.
wiring.printInstruction: After each sprint session: roll d10. On 1–4: advance THE ACCOUNT one mark. On 5–8: add one tally to RESIDUE. On 9–10: advance THE ACCOUNT one mark AND add one tally to RESIDUE. Record both.

wiring.from: ROOM 11 (hotel grid node, final sealed cluster)
wiring.to: THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY — a separate sealed page
wiring.condition: ROOM 11 seal is crossed off (requires TESTIMONY at 10 OR 2 RESIDUE spent on access)
wiring.effect: The LAST PASTORAL ENTRY page unlocks. This page contains the final VIGIL check: player's current VIGIL position determines which of three endings they read. TESTIMONY clock and RESIDUE tally no longer advance — the game has entered its final phase.
wiring.printInstruction: When ROOM 11's seal is crossed off: turn to THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY. Check your VIGIL marker's position now. Do not spend RESIDUE before reading. Read the ending indicated by your current VIGIL position. The PASTORAL RECORD is closed.

weeklyArc.mechanicalFocus: Introduce TESTIMONY fill clock, VIGIL tug-of-war track, RESIDUE resource tally, hotel Facility Grid with all rooms sealed except the lobby. Sprint session introduces THE ACCOUNT heat track passively via the d10 sprint roll.

THE ACCOUNT escalation wire activates — first threshold at 3 is reachable this week. Players feel the VIGIL baseline shift for the first time. TESTIMONY approaches first threshold (4) for Ground Floor Annex unlock.

weeklyArc.narrativePhase: peak — the 90% week mirrors the priest at full certainty before the fracture
weeklyArc.mechanicalFocus: All wires fully active. TESTIMONY likely crosses threshold 2 (7) this week, unlocking the Upper Corridor. THE ACCOUNT under pressure from 90% lifting sessions generating more extreme rolls. VIGIL routing becomes consequential — players may have shifted into permanent -D territory.

weeklyArc.narrativePhase: deload — the 75% reset mirrors the priest holding still, not deciding
weeklyArc.mechanicalFocus: Mechanical deload mirrors workout deload. Lower lifting intensity means fewer TESTIMONY advances. Sprint sessions still fire THE ACCOUNT. The player has accumulated RESIDUE — this week is the prime spending window. VIGIL choices this week echo loudest.

weeklyArc.narrativePhase: return — the 85% surge is the priest moving again
weeklyArc.mechanicalFocus: TESTIMONY likely reaches threshold 3 (10) this week or early week 6, making the ROOM 11 unlock-chain wire live. Players approaching the endgame know they can also force ROOM 11 with 2 RESIDUE — the choice crystallizes: spend faith now, or wait for testimony to accumulate.

weeklyArc.narrativePhase: reckoning — the 95% single-rep week is the priest's only remaining act of will
weeklyArc.mechanicalFocus: ROOM 11 accessible. THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY sealed page becomes available. No new mechanics — the game collapses inward to a single VIGIL reading. The weight of every RESIDUE decision from weeks 1-5 resolves in one final routing check.

categoriesExcluded.reason: The cosmic thing does not respond to cleverness or preparation. There are no bonuses, no push mechanics, no rerolls. The d10 rolls are looked up, not modified. Modifiers would imply the player has leverage over outcomes — they do not.

There is no meta-economy of session setup or teardown rituals. The PASTORAL RECORD is always open. The player enters the hotel and reads — no procedural framing is needed because the 'we' of the narrative voice already handles immersion without mechanical on-ramps.

_designNotes.workoutAsEngine: Lifting sessions advance TESTIMONY (the congregation accumulating witness). Sprint sessions roll against THE ACCOUNT (the thing in the hotel, indifferent, environmental). The 90% peak of week 3 is the moment before the priest's certainty breaks — the highest TESTIMONY and highest ACCOUNT pressure occurring simultaneously. The week 4 deload is mechanical silence: the priest waits. The week 6 single-rep maximum is the only remaining act — ROOM 11, THE LAST PASTORAL ENTRY, the VIGIL reading.
_designNotes.visualArchetype: confessional — the booklet is a parish record that has been interfered with. Someone has crossed out dates. Someone has added margin notes in a different hand. The stamps are ink-heavy and slightly off-register. The paper has been folded and unfolded many times.

diegeticNames.facilityGrid: THE HOTEL — PASTORAL REGISTER OF ROOMS

antiIsomorphismFingerprint.primaryWireType: feedback-loop as decision engine (not threshold-gate as primary)
